002158r 3                               ; Get the number and limit back off the stack.  Rather than
002158r 3                               ; waste time making room on the stack, just use tmp1 and tmp2.
002158r 3               
002158r 3                               ; Get the screen we just loaded.
002158r 3  68                           pla
002159r 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
00215Br 3  68                           pla
00215Cr 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
00215Er 3               
00215Er 3                               ; Get the ending screen.
00215Er 3  68                           pla
00215Fr 3  85 26                        sta tmp2
002161r 3  68                           pla
002162r 3  85 27                        sta tmp2+1
002164r 3               
002164r 3                               ; See if we just loaded the last screen.
002164r 3                               ; A already has the MSB of the last screen in it.
002164r 3  C5 25                        cmp tmp1+1
002166r 3  D0 08                        bne _next_screen
002168r 3  A5 26                        lda tmp2        ; Compare the LSB
00216Ar 3  C5 24                        cmp tmp1
00216Cr 3  D0 02                        bne _next_screen
00216Er 3  80 18                        bra _done       ; We just did the last screen.
002170r 3               
002170r 3               _next_screen:
002170r 3                               ; Put the ending screen back on the data stack.
002170r 3  A5 27                        lda tmp2+1
002172r 3  48                           pha
002173r 3  A5 26                        lda tmp2
002175r 3  48                           pha
002176r 3               
002176r 3                               ; Increment the current screen.
002176r 3  E6 24                        inc tmp1
002178r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
00217Ar 3  E6 25                        inc tmp1+1
00217Cr 3               :
00217Cr 3                               ; Put the current screen on the stack to prepare for
00217Cr 3                               ; the next loop.
00217Cr 3  CA                           dex
00217Dr 3  CA                           dex
00217Er 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1
002180r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002182r 3  A5 25                        lda tmp1+1
002184r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
002186r 3  80 C7                        bra _thru_loop
002188r 3               _done:
002188r 3               .endscope
002188r 3  60           z_thru:         rts
002189r 3               
002189r 3               
002189r 3               ; ## TICK ( "name" -- xt ) "Return a word's execution token (xt)"
002189r 3               ; ## "'"  auto  ANS core
002189r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/Tick"""
002189r 3               xt_tick:
002189r 3               .scope
002189r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_parse_name       ; ( -- addr u )
00218Cr 3               
00218Cr 3                               ; if we got a zero, there was a problem getting the
00218Cr 3                               ; name of the word
00218Cr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00218Er 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
002190r 3  D0 05                        bne :+
002192r 3               
002192r 3  A9 05                        lda #err_noname
002194r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp error
002197r 3               :
002197r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_find_name        ; ( addr u -- nt )
00219Ar 3               
00219Ar 3                               ; If we didn't find the word in the Dictionary, abort
00219Ar 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00219Cr 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00219Er 3  D0 05                        bne :+
0021A0r 3               
0021A0r 3  A9 08                        lda #err_syntax
0021A2r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp error
0021A5r 3               :
0021A5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_name_to_int      ; ( nt -- xt )
0021A8r 3               
0021A8r 3               .endscope
0021A8r 3  60           z_tick:         rts
0021A9r 3               
0021A9r 3               
0021A9r 3               ; ## TO ( n "name" -- ) or ( "name") "Change a value"
0021A9r 3               ; ## "to"  auto  ANS core ext
0021A9r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TO
0021A9r 3                       ; Gives a new value to a, uh, VALUE.
0021A9r 3                       ;
0021A9r 3                       ; One possible Forth
0021A9r 3                       ; implementation is  ' >BODY !  but given the problems we have
0021A9r 3                       ; with >BODY on STC Forths, we do this the hard way. Since
0021A9r 3                       ; Tali Forth uses the same code for CONSTANTs and VALUEs, you
0021A9r 3                       ; could use this to redefine a CONSTANT, but that is a no-no.
0021A9r 3                       ;
0021A9r 3                       ; Note that the standard has different behaviors for TO depending
0021A9r 3                       ; on the state (https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TO).
0021A9r 3                       ; This makes TO state-dependent (which is bad) and also rather
0021A9r 3                       ; complex (see the Gforth implementation for comparison). This
0021A9r 3                       ; word may not be natively compiled and must be immediate. Frankly,
0021A9r 3                       ; it would have made more sense to have two words for this.
0021A9r 3                       ; """
0021A9r 3               xt_to:
0021A9r 3               .scope
0021A9r 3                               ; One way or the other, we need the xt of the word after this
0021A9r 3                               ; one. At this point, we don't know if we are interpreted or
0021A9r 3                               ; compile, so we don't know if there is a value n on the stack,
0021A9r 3                               ; so we can't do an underflow check yet
0021A9r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_tick             ; ( [n] xt )
0021ACr 3               
0021ACr 3                               ; The PFA (DFA in this case) is three bytes down,
0021ACr 3                               ; after the jump to DOCONST
0021ACr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x                 ; LSB
0021AEr 3  18                           clc
0021AFr 3  69 03                        adc #3
0021B1r 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
0021B3r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x                 ; MSB
0021B5r 3  69 00                        adc #0                  ; we just want the carry
0021B7r 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
0021B9r 3               
0021B9r 3  E8                           inx
0021BAr 3  E8                           inx                     ; ( [n] )
0021BBr 3               
0021BBr 3                               ; Now it gets ugly. See which state we are in
0021BBr 3  A5 16                        lda state
0021BDr 3  05 17                        ora state+1
0021BFr 3  F0 34                        beq _interpret
0021C1r 3               
0021C1r 3                               ; Well, we're compiling. We want to end up with simple
0021C1r 3                               ; code that just takes the number that is TOS and saves
0021C1r 3                               ; it in the address of the xt we were just given. So we
0021C1r 3                               ; want to compile this routine:
0021C1r 3                               ;
0021C1r 3                               ;       lda 0,x                 - B5 00
0021C1r 3                               ;       sta <ADDR_LSB>          - 8D LSB MSB
0021C1r 3                               ;       lda 1,x                 - B5 01
0021C1r 3                               ;       sta <ADDR_LSB>          - 8D LSB MSB
0021C1r 3                               ;       inx                     - E8
0021C1r 3                               ;       inx                     - E8
0021C1r 3                               ;
0021C1r 3                               ; which at least is nice and short. Other than that, we pretty
0021C1r 3                               ; much have to do this the hard and long way, because with the
0021C1r 3                               ; LSBs and MSBs, we can't really put the numbers in a data
0021C1r 3                               ; range and store them with a loop. Sigh.
0021C1r 3               
0021C1r 3  A0 00                        ldy #$00                ; Code for LDA 0,X
0021C3r 3  A9 B5                        lda #$B5
0021C5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_word
0021C8r 3               
0021C8r 3  A9 8D                        lda #$8D                ; Code for STA abs
0021CAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_a
0021CDr 3               
0021CDr 3  A4 25                        ldy tmp1+1              ; MSB goes in Y
0021CFr 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1
0021D1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_word
0021D4r 3               
0021D4r 3  A0 01                        ldy #$01                ; Code for LDA 1,X
0021D6r 3  A9 B5                        lda #$B5
0021D8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_word
0021DBr 3               
0021DBr 3  A9 8D                        lda #$8D                ; Code for STA abs
0021DDr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_a
0021E0r 3               
0021E0r 3  E6 24                        inc tmp1                ; Calculate MSB
0021E2r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
0021E4r 3  E6 25                        inc tmp1+1
0021E6r 3               :
0021E6r 3  A4 25                        ldy tmp1+1              ; MSB goes in Y
0021E8r 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1
0021EAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_word
0021EDr 3               
0021EDr 3  A0 E8                        ldy #$E8                ; Code for INX
0021EFr 3  98                           tya
0021F0r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_word
0021F3r 3               
0021F3r 3  80 0F                        bra _done
0021F5r 3               
0021F5r 3               _interpret:
0021F5r 3                               ; We're interpreting, so we arrive here with n
0021F5r 3                               ; on the stack. This is an annoying place to put
0021F5r 3                               ; the underflow check because we can't
0021F5r 3                               ; automatically strip it out
0021F5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
0021F8r 3               
0021F8r 3                               ; We skip over the jump to DOCONST and store the number
0021F8r 3                               ; in the Program Field Area (PDF, in this case more a
0021F8r 3                               ; Data Field Area
0021F8r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0021FAr 3  92 24                        sta (tmp1)              ; LSB
0021FCr 3               
0021FCr 3  A0 01                        ldy #1
0021FEr 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x                 ; MSB
002200r 3  91 24                        sta (tmp1),y            ; fall through to common
002202r 3               
002202r 3  E8                           inx                     ; DROP
002203r 3  E8                           inx
002204r 3               _done:
002204r 3               .endscope
002204r 3  60           z_to:           rts
002205r 3               
002205r 3               
002205r 3               ; ## TO_BODY ( xt -- addr ) "Return a word's Code Field Area (CFA)"
002205r 3               ; ## ">body"  auto  ANS core
002205r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/toBODY
002205r 3                       ; Given a word's execution token (xt), return the address of the
002205r 3                       ; start of that word's parameter field (PFA). This is defined as the
002205r 3                       ; address that HERE would return right after CREATE.
002205r 3                       ;
002205r 3                       ; This is a
002205r 3                       ; difficult word for STC Forths, because most words don't actually
002205r 3                       ; have a Code Field Area (CFA) to skip. We solve this by having CREATE
002205r 3                       ; add a flag, "has CFA" (HC), in the header so >BODY know to skip
002205r 3                       ; the subroutine jumps to DOVAR, DOCONST, or DODOES
002205r 3                       ; """
002205r 3               xt_to_body:
002205r 3               .scope
002205r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
002208r 3               
002208r 3                               ; Ideally, xt already points to the CFA. We just need to check
002208r 3                               ; the HC flag for special cases
002208r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; ( xt xt )
00220Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_int_to_name      ; ( xt nt )
00220Er 3               
00220Er 3                               ; The status byte is nt+1
00220Er 3  F6 00                        inc 0,x
002210r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
002212r 3  F6 01                        inc 1,x
002214r 3               :
002214r 3  A1 00                        lda (0,x)               ; get status byte
002216r 3  29 20                        and #HC
002218r 3  F0 0D                        beq _no_cfa
00221Ar 3               
00221Ar 3                               ; We've got a DOVAR, DOCONST, DODEFER, DODOES or whatever,
00221Ar 3                               ; so we add three to xt, which is NOS
00221Ar 3  18                           clc
00221Br 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x         ; LSB
00221Dr 3  69 03                        adc #3
00221Fr 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
002221r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x         ; MSB
002223r 3  69 00                        adc #0          ; we conly care about the carry
002225r 3  95 03                        sta 3,x         ; Fall through to _no_cfa
002227r 3               _no_cfa:
002227r 3  E8                           inx             ; get rid of the nt
002228r 3  E8                           inx
002229r 3               _done:
002229r 3               .endscope
002229r 3  60           z_to_body:      rts
00222Ar 3               
00222Ar 3               
00222Ar 3               ; ## TO_IN ( -- addr ) "Return address of the input pointer"
00222Ar 3               ; ## ">in"  auto  ANS core
00222Ar 3               xt_to_in:
00222Ar 3  CA                           dex
00222Br 3  CA                           dex
00222Cr 3               
00222Cr 3  A9 0C                        lda #<toin
00222Er 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002230r 3  A9 00                        lda #>toin      ; paranoid, should be zero
002232r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
002234r 3               
002234r 3  60           z_to_in:        rts
002235r 3               
002235r 3               
002235r 3               ; ## TO_NUMBER ( ud addr u -- ud addr u ) "Convert a number"
002235r 3               ; ## ">number"  auto  ANS core
002235r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/toNUMBER
002235r 3                       ; Convert a string to a double number. Logic here is based on the
002235r 3                       ; routine by Phil Burk of the same name in pForth, see
002235r 3                       ; https://github.com/philburk/pforth/blob/master/fth/numberio.fth
002235r 3                       ; for the original Forth code. We arrive here from NUMBER which has
002235r 3                       ; made sure that we don't have to deal with a sign and we don't have
002235r 3                       ; to deal with a dot as a last character that signalizes double -
002235r 3                       ; this should be a pure number string.
002235r 3                       ;
002235r 3                       ; This routine calles UM*, which uses tmp1, tmp2 and tmp3, so we
002235r 3                       ; cannot access any of those.
002235r 3                       ;
002235r 3                       ; For the math routine, we move the inputs to the scratchpad to
002235r 3                       ; avoid having to fool around with the Data Stack.
002235r 3                       ;
002235r 3                       ;     +-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+
002235r 3                       ;     |   UD-LO   |   UD-HI   |     N     | UD-HI-LO  |
002235r 3                       ;     |           |           |           |           |
002235r 3                       ;     |  S    S+1 | S+2   S+3 | S+4   S+5 | S+6   S+7 |
002235r 3                       ;     +-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+-----+
002235r 3                       ;
002235r 3                       ; The math routine works by converting one character to its
002235r 3                       ; numerical value (N) via DIGIT? and storing it in S+4 for
002235r 3                       ; the moment. We then multiply the UD-HI value with the radix
002235r 3                       ; (from BASE) using UM*, which returns a double-cell result. We
002235r 3                       ; discard the high cell of that result (UD-HI-HI) and store the
002235r 3                       ; low cell (UD-HI-LO) in S+6 for now. -- The second part is
002235r 3                       ; multiplying UD-LO with the radix. The high cell (UD-LO-HI)
002235r 3                       ; gets put in S+2, the low cell (HD-LO-LO) in S. We then use
002235r 3                       ; a version of D+ to add ( S S+2 ) and ( S+4 S+6) together,
002235r 3                       ; storing the result back in S and S+2, before we start another
002235r 3                       ; round with it as the new UD-LO and UD-HI.
002235r 3                       ; """
002235r 3               
002235r 3               xt_to_number:
002235r 3               .scope
002235r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_4
002238r 3               
002238r 3                               ; Fill the scratchpad. We arrive with ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ).
002238r 3                               ; After this step, the original ud-lo and ud-hi will still be on
002238r 3                               ; the Data Stack, but will be ignored and later overwritten
002238r 3                               ; If >NUMBER is called by NUMBER, these should be all zeros
002238r 3  B5 06                        lda 6,x         ; ud-lo LSB
00223Ar 3  85 36                        sta scratch
00223Cr 3  B5 07                        lda 7,x         ; ud-lo MSB
00223Er 3  85 37                        sta scratch+1
002240r 3               
002240r 3  B5 04                        lda 4,x         ; ud-hi LSB
002242r 3  85 38                        sta scratch+2
002244r 3  B5 05                        lda 5,x         ; ud-hi MSB
002246r 3  85 39                        sta scratch+3
002248r 3               
002248r 3                               ; Push down one on the Data Stack to use TOS for character
002248r 3                               ; conversion ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u x )
002248r 3  CA                           dex
002249r 3  CA                           dex
00224Ar 3               
00224Ar 3               _loop:
00224Ar 3                               ; Get one character based on address
00224Ar 3  A1 04                        lda (4,x)
00224Cr 3  95 00                        sta 0,x                 ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u char )
00224Er 3  74 01                        stz 1,x                 ; paranoid
002250r 3               
002250r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_digit_question   ; ( char -- n -1 | char 0 )
002253r 3               
002253r 3                               ; This gives us ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u char f | n f ), so we
002253r 3                               ; check the flag. If it is zero, we return what we have and
002253r 3                               ; let the caller (usually NUMBER) complain
002253r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
002255r 3  D0 04                        bne _digit_ok
002257r 3               
002257r 3  E8                           inx
002258r 3  E8                           inx
002259r 3  80 67                        bra _done       ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u char )
00225Br 3               
00225Br 3               _digit_ok:
00225Br 3                               ; Conversion was successful. We arrive here with
00225Br 3                               ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u n -1 ) and can start the
00225Br 3                               ; math routine
00225Br 3               
00225Br 3                               ; Save n so we don't have to fool around with the
00225Br 3                               ; Data Stack
00225Br 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
00225Dr 3  85 3A                        sta scratch+4
00225Fr 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
002261r 3  85 3B                        sta scratch+5
002263r 3               
002263r 3                               ; Now multiply ud-hi (the one in the scratchpad, not the
002263r 3                               ; original one on the Data Stack) with the radix from BASE.
002263r 3                               ; We can clobber TOS and NOS because we saved n
002263r 3  A5 38                        lda scratch+2
002265r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x         ; NOS
002267r 3  A5 39                        lda scratch+3
002269r 3  95 03                        sta 3,x
00226Br 3               
00226Br 3  A5 18                        lda base
00226Dr 3  95 00                        sta 0,x         ; TOS
00226Fr 3  74 01                        stz 1,x         ; now ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ud-hi base)
002271r 3               
002271r 3                               ; UM* returns a double-celled number
002271r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_um_star  ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ud-hi-lo ud-hi-hi )
002274r 3               
002274r 3                               ; Move ud-hi-lo to safety
002274r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x         ; ud-hi-lo
002276r 3  85 3C                        sta scratch+6
002278r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
00227Ar 3  85 3D                        sta scratch+7
00227Cr 3               
00227Cr 3                               ; Now we multiply ud-lo, overwriting the stack entries
00227Cr 3  A5 36                        lda scratch
00227Er 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
002280r 3  A5 37                        lda scratch+1
002282r 3  95 03                        sta 3,x         ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ud-lo ud-hi-hi )
002284r 3               
002284r 3  A5 18                        lda base
002286r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002288r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x         ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ud-lo base )
00228Ar 3               
00228Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_um_star  ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ud-lo-lo ud-lo-hi )
00228Dr 3               
00228Dr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00228Fr 3  85 38                        sta scratch+2
002291r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
002293r 3  85 39                        sta scratch+3
002295r 3               
002295r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
002297r 3  85 36                        sta scratch
002299r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
00229Br 3  85 37                        sta scratch+1
00229Dr 3               
00229Dr 3                               ; We add ud-lo and n, as well as ud-hi and ud-hi-lo,
00229Dr 3                               ; both in the scratch pad
00229Dr 3  18                           clc
00229Er 3  A5 36                        lda scratch     ; ud-lo LSB
0022A0r 3  65 3A                        adc scratch+4   ; n LSB
0022A2r 3  85 36                        sta scratch     ; this is the new ud-lo
0022A4r 3  A5 37                        lda scratch+1   ; ud-lo MSB
0022A6r 3  65 3B                        adc scratch+5   ; n MSB
0022A8r 3  85 37                        sta scratch+1
0022AAr 3               
0022AAr 3  A5 38                        lda scratch+2   ; LSB
0022ACr 3  65 3C                        adc scratch+6
0022AEr 3  85 38                        sta scratch+2   ; this is the new ud-hi
0022B0r 3  A5 39                        lda scratch+3   ; MSB
0022B2r 3  65 3D                        adc scratch+7
0022B4r 3  85 39                        sta scratch+3
0022B6r 3               
0022B6r 3                               ; Clean up: Get rid of one of the two top elements on
0022B6r 3                               ; the Data Stack. We don't really care which one
0022B6r 3  E8                           inx
0022B7r 3  E8                           inx             ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ud-lo-lo )
0022B8r 3               
0022B8r 3                               ; One character down. Move address up
0022B8r 3  F6 04                        inc 4,x
0022BAr 3  D0 02                        bne :+
0022BCr 3  F6 05                        inc 5,x
0022BEr 3               :
0022BEr 3                               ; Decrease counter
0022BEr 3  D6 02                        dec 2,x
0022C0r 3  D0 88                        bne _loop
0022C2r 3               
0022C2r 3               _done:
0022C2r 3                               ; Counter has reached zero or we have an error. In both
0022C2r 3                               ; cases, we clean up the Data Stack and return. Error gives
0022C2r 3                               ; us ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u char ), regular end is
0022C2r 3                               ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u ud-lo )
0022C2r 3  E8                           inx
0022C3r 3  E8                           inx             ; ( ud-lo ud-hi addr u )
0022C4r 3               
0022C4r 3                               ; The new ud-lo and ud-hi are still on the scratch pad
0022C4r 3  A5 36                        lda scratch     ; new ud-lo
0022C6r 3  95 06                        sta 6,x
0022C8r 3  A5 37                        lda scratch+1
0022CAr 3  95 07                        sta 7,x
0022CCr 3               
0022CCr 3  A5 38                        lda scratch+2
0022CEr 3  95 04                        sta 4,x
0022D0r 3  A5 39                        lda scratch+3
0022D2r 3  95 05                        sta 5,x
0022D4r 3               
0022D4r 3               .endscope
0022D4r 3  60           z_to_number:    rts
0022D5r 3               
0022D5r 3               
0022D5r 3               ; ## TO_ORDER ( wid -- ) "Add wordlist at beginning of search order"
0022D5r 3               ; ## ">order"  tested  Gforth search
0022D5r 3                       ; """https://www.complang.tuwien.ac.at/forth/gforth/Docs-html/Word-Lists.html"""
0022D5r 3               xt_to_order:
0022D5r 3                               ; Put the wid on the return stack for now.
0022D5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_to_r
0022D8r 3               
0022D8r 3                               ; Get the current search order.
0022D8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_get_order
0022DBr 3               
0022DBr 3                               ; Get back the wid and add it to the list.
0022DBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_r_from
0022DEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap
0022E1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus
0022E4r 3               
0022E4r 3                               ; Set the search order with the new list.
0022E4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_set_order
0022E7r 3               
0022E7r 3  60           z_to_order:     rts
0022E8r 3               
0022E8r 3               
0022E8r 3               ; ## TO_R ( n -- )(R: -- n) "Push TOS to the Return Stack"
0022E8r 3               ; ## ">r"  auto  ANS core
0022E8r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/toR
0022E8r 3                       ; This word is handled differently for native and for
0022E8r 3                       ; subroutine coding, see `COMPILE,`. This is a complile-only
0022E8r 3                       ; word.
0022E8r 3                       ; """
0022E8r 3               xt_to_r:
0022E8r 3                               ; Save the return address. If this word is natively
0022E8r 3                               ; coded, this is a complete waste of cycles, but
0022E8r 3                               ; required for subroutine coding
0022E8r 3  68                           pla             ; LSB
0022E9r 3  85 2C                        sta tmptos
0022EBr 3  7A                           ply             ; MSB
0022ECr 3               
0022ECr 3                               ; --- CUT HERE FOR NATIVE CODING ---
0022ECr 3               
0022ECr 3                               ; We check for underflow in the second step, so we can
0022ECr 3                               ; strip off the stack thrashing for native compiling first
0022ECr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
0022EFr 3               
0022EFr 3                               ; now we can do the actual work
0022EFr 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x         ; MSB
0022F1r 3  48                           pha
0022F2r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x         ; LSB
0022F4r 3  48                           pha
0022F5r 3               
0022F5r 3  E8                           inx
0022F6r 3  E8                           inx
0022F7r 3               
0022F7r 3                               ; --- CUT HERE FOR NATIVE CODING ---
0022F7r 3               
0022F7r 3                               ; restore return address
0022F7r 3  5A                           phy             ; MSB
0022F8r 3  A5 2C                        lda tmptos
0022FAr 3  48                           pha             ; LSB
0022FBr 3               
0022FBr 3  60           z_to_r:         rts
0022FCr 3               
0022FCr 3               
0022FCr 3               ; ## TRUE ( -- f ) "Push TRUE flag to Data Stack"
0022FCr 3               ; ## "true"  auto  ANS core ext
0022FCr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TRUE"""
0022FCr 3               xt_true:
0022FCr 3  CA                           dex
0022FDr 3  CA                           dex
0022FEr 3  A9 FF                        lda #$FF
002300r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002302r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
002304r 3               
002304r 3  60           z_true:         rts
002305r 3               
002305r 3               
002305r 3               ; ## TUCK ( b a -- a b a ) "Copy TOS below NOS"
002305r 3               ; ## "tuck"  auto  ANS core ext
002305r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TUCK"""
002305r 3               xt_tuck:
002305r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
002308r 3               
002308r 3  CA                           dex
002309r 3  CA                           dex
00230Ar 3               
00230Ar 3  B4 04                        ldy 4,x         ; LSB
00230Cr 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
00230Er 3  95 04                        sta 4,x
002310r 3  94 02                        sty 2,x
002312r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002314r 3               
002314r 3  B4 05                        ldy 5,x         ; MSB
002316r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
002318r 3  95 05                        sta 5,x
00231Ar 3  94 03                        sty 3,x         ; bba
00231Cr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x         ; baa
00231Er 3               
00231Er 3  60           z_tuck:         rts
00231Fr 3               
00231Fr 3               
00231Fr 3               ; ## TWO ( -- u ) "Push the number 2 to stack"
00231Fr 3               ; ## "2"  auto  Tali Forth
00231Fr 3                       ;
00231Fr 3                       ; This code is shared with ASSEMBLER-WORDLIST
00231Fr 3               xt_assembler_wordlist:
00231Fr 3               xt_two:
00231Fr 3  CA                           dex
002320r 3  CA                           dex
002321r 3  A9 02                        lda #2
002323r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002325r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
002327r 3               
002327r 3               z_assembler_wordlist:
002327r 3  60           z_two:          rts
002328r 3               
002328r 3               
002328r 3               ; ## TWO_DROP ( n n -- ) "Drop TOS and NOS"
002328r 3               ; ## "2drop"  auto  ANS core
002328r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoDROP"""
002328r 3               xt_two_drop:
002328r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
00232Br 3               
00232Br 3  E8                           inx
00232Cr 3  E8                           inx
00232Dr 3  E8                           inx
00232Er 3  E8                           inx
00232Fr 3               
00232Fr 3  60           z_two_drop:     rts
002330r 3               
002330r 3               
002330r 3               ; ## TWO_DUP ( a b -- a b a b ) "Duplicate first two stack elements"
002330r 3               ; ## "2dup"  auto  ANS core
002330r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoDUP"""
002330r 3               xt_two_dup:
002330r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
002333r 3               
002333r 3  CA                           dex
002334r 3  CA                           dex
002335r 3  CA                           dex
002336r 3  CA                           dex
002337r 3               
002337r 3  B5 04                        lda 4,x         ; TOS
002339r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00233Br 3  B5 05                        lda 5,x
00233Dr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00233Fr 3               
00233Fr 3  B5 06                        lda 6,x         ; NOS
002341r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
002343r 3  B5 07                        lda 7,x
002345r 3  95 03                        sta 3,x
002347r 3               
002347r 3  60           z_two_dup:      rts
002348r 3               
002348r 3               
002348r 3               ; ## TWO_FETCH ( addr -- n1 n2 ) "Fetch the cell pair n1 n2 stored at addr"
002348r 3               ; ## "2@"  auto  ANS core
002348r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoFetch
002348r 3                       ; Note n2 stored at addr and n1 in the next cell -- in our case,
002348r 3                       ; the next two bytes. This is equvalent to  `DUP CELL+ @ SWAP @`
002348r 3                       ; """
002348r 3               xt_two_fetch:
002348r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
00234Br 3               
00234Br 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00234Dr 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
00234Fr 3  B4 01                        ldy 1,x
002351r 3  84 25                        sty tmp1+1
002353r 3               
002353r 3  CA                           dex             ; reuse one stack element
002354r 3  CA                           dex
002355r 3               
002355r 3  B2 24                        lda (tmp1)      ; copy LSB
002357r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002359r 3  A0 01                        ldy #1          ; copy next
00235Br 3  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y
00235Dr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00235Fr 3  C8                           iny             ; copy next
002360r 3  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y
002362r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
002364r 3  C8                           iny             ; copy next
002365r 3  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y
002367r 3  95 03                        sta 3,x
002369r 3               
002369r 3  60           z_two_fetch:    rts
00236Ar 3               
00236Ar 3               ; ## TWO_OVER ( d1 d2 -- d1 d2 d1 ) "Copy double word NOS to TOS"
00236Ar 3               ; ## "2over"  auto  ANS core
00236Ar 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoOVER"""
00236Ar 3               xt_two_over:
00236Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_4
00236Dr 3               
00236Dr 3  CA                           dex
00236Er 3  CA                           dex
00236Fr 3  CA                           dex
002370r 3  CA                           dex
002371r 3               
002371r 3  B5 08                        lda 8,x
002373r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002375r 3               
002375r 3  B5 09                        lda 9,x
002377r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
002379r 3               
002379r 3  B5 0A                        lda 10,x
00237Br 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
00237Dr 3               
00237Dr 3  B5 0B                        lda 11,x
00237Fr 3  95 03                        sta 3,x
002381r 3               
002381r 3  60           z_two_over:     rts
002382r 3               
002382r 3               
002382r 3               ; ## TWO_R_FETCH ( -- n n ) "Copy top two entries from Return Stack"
002382r 3               ; ## "2r@"  auto  ANS core ext
002382r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoRFetch
002382r 3                       ;
002382r 3                       ; This is R> R> 2DUP >R >R SWAP but we can do it a lot faster in
002382r 3                       ; assembler. We use trickery to access the elements on the Return
002382r 3                       ; Stack instead of pulling the return address first and storing
002382r 3                       ; it somewhere else like for 2R> and 2>R. In this version, we leave
002382r 3                       ; it as Never Native; at some point, we should compare versions to
002382r 3                       ; see if an Always Native version would be better
002382r 3                       ; """
002382r 3               xt_two_r_fetch:
002382r 3               		; make room on the Data Stack
002382r 3  CA                           dex
002383r 3  CA                           dex
002384r 3  CA                           dex
002385r 3  CA                           dex
002386r 3               
002386r 3                               ; Get four bytes off of Return Stack. This assumes that
002386r 3                               ; we took a subroutine jump here so the first two entries
002386r 3                               ; are the return address
002386r 3  8A                           txa
002387r 3  BA                           tsx
002388r 3  DA                           phx             ; 65c02 has no TXY, so do it the hard way
002389r 3  7A                           ply
00238Ar 3  AA                           tax
00238Br 3               
00238Br 3                               ; The Return Stack addreses $0101 and $0102 are occupied by
00238Br 3                               ; the return address for this word. This is a whole lot
00238Br 3                               ; easier on the 65816
00238Br 3  B9 03 01                     lda $0103,y     ; LSB of top entry
00238Er 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002390r 3  B9 04 01                     lda $0104,y     ; MSB of top entry
002393r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
002395r 3  B9 05 01                     lda $0105,y     ; LSB of bottom entry
002398r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
00239Ar 3  B9 06 01                     lda $0106,y     ; MSB of top entry
00239Dr 3  95 03                        sta 3,x
00239Fr 3               
00239Fr 3  60           z_two_r_fetch:  rts
0023A0r 3               
0023A0r 3               
0023A0r 3               ; ## TWO_R_FROM ( -- n1 n2 ) (R: n1 n2 -- ) "Pull two cells from Return Stack"
0023A0r 3               ; ## "2r>"  auto  ANS core ext
0023A0r 3               	    ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoRfrom
0023A0r 3                       ; Pull top two entries from Return Stack.
0023A0r 3                       ;
0023A0r 3                       ; Is the same as
0023A0r 3                       ; R> R> SWAP. As with R>, the problem with the is word is that
0023A0r 3                       ; the top value on the ReturnStack for a STC Forth is the
0023A0r 3                       ; return address, which we need to get out of the way first.
0023A0r 3                       ; Native compile needs to be handled as a special case.
0023A0r 3                       ; """
0023A0r 3               xt_two_r_from:
0023A0r 3                               ; save the return address
0023A0r 3  68                           pla                     ; LSB
0023A1r 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
0023A3r 3  68                           pla                     ; MSB
0023A4r 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
0023A6r 3               
0023A6r 3                               ; --- CUT HERE FOR NATIVE CODING ---
0023A6r 3               
0023A6r 3               		; make room on stack
0023A6r 3  CA                           dex
0023A7r 3  CA                           dex
0023A8r 3  CA                           dex
0023A9r 3  CA                           dex
0023AAr 3               
0023AAr 3                               ; In theory, we should test for underflow on the Return
0023AAr 3                               ; Stack. However, given the traffic there with an STC
0023AAr 3                               ; Forth, that's probably not really useful
0023AAr 3               
0023AAr 3                               ; now we can access the data
0023AAr 3  68                           pla                     ; LSB
0023ABr 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0023ADr 3  68                           pla                     ; MSB
0023AEr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0023B0r 3               
0023B0r 3  68                           pla                     ; LSB
0023B1r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
0023B3r 3  68                           pla                     ; MSB
0023B4r 3  95 03                        sta 3,x
0023B6r 3               
0023B6r 3                               ; --- CUT HERE FOR NATIVE CODING ---
0023B6r 3               
0023B6r 3                               ; restore return address
0023B6r 3  A5 25                        lda tmp1+1              ; MSB
0023B8r 3  48                           pha
0023B9r 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1                ; LSB
0023BBr 3  48                           pha
0023BCr 3               
0023BCr 3  60           z_two_r_from:   rts
0023BDr 3               
0023BDr 3               
0023BDr 3               ; ## TWO_SLASH ( n -- n ) "Divide TOS by two"
0023BDr 3               ; ## "2/"  auto  ANS core
0023BDr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoDiv"""
0023BDr 3               xt_two_slash:
0023BDr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
0023C0r 3               
0023C0r 3                               ; We can't just LSR the LSB and ROR the MSB because that
0023C0r 3                               ; would do bad things to the sign
0023C0r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
0023C2r 3  0A                           asl                     ; save the sign
0023C3r 3  76 01                        ror 1,x
0023C5r 3  76 00                        ror 0,x
0023C7r 3               
0023C7r 3  60           z_two_slash:    rts
0023C8r 3               
0023C8r 3               
0023C8r 3               ; ## TWO_STAR ( n -- n ) "Multiply TOS by two"
0023C8r 3               ; ## "2*"  auto  ANS core
0023C8r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoTimes
0023C8r 3                       ;
0023C8r 3                       ; Also used for CELLS
0023C8r 3                       ; """
0023C8r 3               xt_two_star:
0023C8r 3               xt_cells:
0023C8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
0023CBr 3               
0023CBr 3  16 00                        asl 0,x
0023CDr 3  36 01                        rol 1,x
0023CFr 3               z_cells:
0023CFr 3  60           z_two_star:     rts
0023D0r 3               
0023D0r 3               
0023D0r 3               ; ## TWO_STORE ( n1 n2 addr -- ) "Store two numbers at given address"
0023D0r 3               ; ## "2!"  auto  ANS core
0023D0r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoStore
0023D0r 3                       ; Stores so n2 goes to addr and n1 to the next consecutive cell.
0023D0r 3                       ; Is equivalent to  `SWAP OVER ! CELL+ !`
0023D0r 3                       ; """
0023D0r 3               xt_two_store:
0023D0r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_3
0023D3r 3               
0023D3r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0023D5r 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
0023D7r 3  B4 01                        ldy 1,x
0023D9r 3  84 25                        sty tmp1+1
0023DBr 3               
0023DBr 3  E8                           inx
0023DCr 3  E8                           inx
0023DDr 3               
0023DDr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x         ; copy MSB
0023DFr 3  92 24                        sta (tmp1)
0023E1r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x         ; copy next
0023E3r 3  A0 01                        ldy #1
0023E5r 3  91 24                        sta (tmp1),y
0023E7r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x         ; copy next
0023E9r 3  C8                           iny
0023EAr 3  91 24                        sta (tmp1),y
0023ECr 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x         ; copy MSB
0023EEr 3  C8                           iny
0023EFr 3  91 24                        sta (tmp1),y
0023F1r 3               
0023F1r 3  E8                           inx             ; 2DROP
0023F2r 3  E8                           inx
0023F3r 3  E8                           inx
0023F4r 3  E8                           inx
0023F5r 3               
0023F5r 3  60           z_two_store:    rts
0023F6r 3               
0023F6r 3               
0023F6r 3               ; ## TWO_SWAP ( n1 n2 n3 n4 -- n3 n4 n1 n1 ) "Exchange two double words"
0023F6r 3               ; ## "2swap"  auto  ANS core
0023F6r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwoSWAP"""
0023F6r 3               xt_two_swap:
0023F6r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_4
0023F9r 3               
0023F9r 3                               ; 0 <-> 4
0023F9r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0023FBr 3  B4 04                        ldy 4,x
0023FDr 3  95 04                        sta 4,x
0023FFr 3  94 00                        sty 0,x
002401r 3               
002401r 3                               ; 1 <-> 5
002401r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
002403r 3  B4 05                        ldy 5,x
002405r 3  95 05                        sta 5,x
002407r 3  94 01                        sty 1,x
002409r 3               
002409r 3                               ; 2 <-> 6
002409r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
00240Br 3  B4 06                        ldy 6,x
00240Dr 3  95 06                        sta 6,x
00240Fr 3  94 02                        sty 2,x
002411r 3               
002411r 3                               ; 3 <-> 7
002411r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
002413r 3  B4 07                        ldy 7,x
002415r 3  95 07                        sta 7,x
002417r 3  94 03                        sty 3,x
002419r 3               
002419r 3  60           z_two_swap:     rts
00241Ar 3               
00241Ar 3               
00241Ar 3               ; ## TWO_TO_R ( n1 n2 -- )(R: -- n1 n2 "Push top two entries to Return Stack"
00241Ar 3               ; ## "2>r"  auto  ANS core ext
00241Ar 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TwotoR
00241Ar 3                       ; Push top two entries to Return Stack.
00241Ar 3                       ;
00241Ar 3                       ; The same as SWAP >R >R
00241Ar 3                       ; except that if we jumped here, the return address will be in the
00241Ar 3                       ; way. May not be natively compiled unless we're clever and use
00241Ar 3                       ; special routines.
00241Ar 3                       ; """
00241Ar 3               xt_two_to_r:
00241Ar 3                               ; save the return address
00241Ar 3  68                           pla             ; LSB
00241Br 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
00241Dr 3  68                           pla             ; MSB
00241Er 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
002420r 3               
002420r 3                               ; --- CUT HERE FOR NATIVE CODING ---
002420r 3               
002420r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
002423r 3               
002423r 3                               ; now we can move the data
002423r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x         ; MSB
002425r 3  48                           pha
002426r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x         ; LSB
002428r 3  48                           pha
002429r 3               
002429r 3                               ; now we can move the data
002429r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x         ; MSB
00242Br 3  48                           pha
00242Cr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x         ; LSB
00242Er 3  48                           pha
00242Fr 3               
00242Fr 3  E8                           inx
002430r 3  E8                           inx
002431r 3  E8                           inx
002432r 3  E8                           inx
002433r 3               
002433r 3                               ; --- CUT HERE FOR NATIVE CODING ---
002433r 3               
002433r 3                               ; restore return address
002433r 3  A5 25                        lda tmp1+1      ; MSB
002435r 3  48                           pha
002436r 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1        ; LSB
002438r 3  48                           pha
002439r 3               
002439r 3  60           z_two_to_r:     rts
00243Ar 3               
00243Ar 3               
00243Ar 3               ; ## TWO_CONSTANT (C: d "name" -- ) ( -- d) "Create a constant for a double word"
00243Ar 3               ; ## "2constant"  auto  ANS double
00243Ar 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/double/TwoCONSTANT
00243Ar 3                       ;
00243Ar 3                       ; Based on the Forth code
00243Ar 3                       ; : 2CONSTANT ( D -- )  CREATE SWAP , , DOES> DUP @ SWAP CELL+ @ ;
00243Ar 3                       ; """
00243Ar 3               xt_two_constant:
00243Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
00243Dr 3               
00243Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_create
002440r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap
002443r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_comma
002446r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_comma
002449r 3               
002449r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr does_runtime    ; does> turns into these two routines.
00244Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr dodoes
00244Fr 3               
00244Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup
002452r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch
002455r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap
002458r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cell_plus
00245Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch
00245Er 3               
00245Er 3  60           z_two_constant: rts
00245Fr 3               
00245Fr 3               
00245Fr 3               ; ## TWO_LITERAL (C: d -- ) ( -- d) "Compile a literal double word"
00245Fr 3               ; ## "2literal"  auto  ANS double
00245Fr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/double/TwoLITERAL"""
00245Fr 3                       ; Based on the Forth code
00245Fr 3                       ; : 2LITERAL ( D -- ) SWAP POSTPONE LITERAL POSTPONE LITERAL ; IMMEDIATE
00245Fr 3                       ; """
00245Fr 3               xt_two_literal:
00245Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2 ; double number
002462r 3               
002462r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap
002465r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_literal
002468r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_literal
00246Br 3               
00246Br 3  60           z_two_literal:  rts
00246Cr 3               
00246Cr 3               
00246Cr 3               ; ## TWO_VARIABLE ( "name" -- ) "Create a variable for a double word"
00246Cr 3               ; ## "2variable"  auto  ANS double
00246Cr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/double/TwoVARIABLE
00246Cr 3                       ; The variable is not initialized to zero.
00246Cr 3                       ;
00246Cr 3                       ; This can be realized in Forth as either
00246Cr 3                       ; CREATE 2 CELLS ALLOT  or just  CREATE 0 , 0 ,
00246Cr 3                       ; """
00246Cr 3               xt_two_variable:
00246Cr 3                               ; We just let CRATE and ALLOT do the heavy lifting
00246Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_create
00246Fr 3               
00246Fr 3  CA                           dex
002470r 3  CA                           dex
002471r 3  A9 04                        lda #4
002473r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002475r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
002477r 3               
002477r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_allot
00247Ar 3               
00247Ar 3  60           z_two_variable: rts
00247Br 3               
00247Br 3               
00247Br 3               ; ## TYPE ( addr u -- ) "Print string"
00247Br 3               ; ## "type"  auto  ANS core
00247Br 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/TYPE
00247Br 3                       ; Works through EMIT to allow OUTPUT revectoring.
00247Br 3                       ; """
00247Br 3               xt_type:
00247Br 3               .scope
00247Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
00247Er 3               
00247Er 3                               ; Save the starting address into tmp1
00247Er 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
002480r 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
002482r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
002484r 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
002486r 3               _loop:
002486r 3                               ; done if length is zero
002486r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
002488r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00248Ar 3  F0 15                        beq _done
00248Cr 3               
00248Cr 3                               ; Send the current character
00248Cr 3  B2 24                        lda (tmp1)
00248Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr emit_a      ; avoids stack foolery
002491r 3               
002491r 3                               ; Move the address along (in tmp1)
002491r 3  E6 24                        inc tmp1
002493r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
002495r 3  E6 25                        inc tmp1+1
002497r 3               :
002497r 3                               ; Reduce the count (on the data stack)
002497r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
002499r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
00249Br 3  D6 01                        dec 1,x
00249Dr 3               :
00249Dr 3  D6 00                        dec 0,x
00249Fr 3               
00249Fr 3  80 E5                        bra _loop
0024A1r 3               _done:
0024A1r 3  E8                           inx
0024A2r 3  E8                           inx
0024A3r 3  E8                           inx
0024A4r 3  E8                           inx
0024A5r 3               
0024A5r 3               .endscope
0024A5r 3  60           z_type:         rts
0024A6r 3               
0024A6r 3               
0024A6r 3               ; ## U_DOT ( u -- ) "Print TOS as unsigned number"
0024A6r 3               ; ## "u."  tested  ANS core
0024A6r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/Ud
0024A6r 3                       ;
0024A6r 3                       ; This is : U. 0 <# #S #> TYPE SPACE ; in Forth
0024A6r 3                       ; We use the internal assembler function print_u followed
0024A6r 3                       ; by a single space
0024A6r 3                       ; """
0024A6r 3               xt_u_dot:
0024A6r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
0024A9r 3               
0024A9r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr print_u
0024ACr 3  A9 20                        lda #AscSP
0024AEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr emit_a
0024B1r 3               
0024B1r 3  60           z_u_dot:        rts
0024B2r 3               
0024B2r 3               
0024B2r 3               ; ## U_DOT_R ( u u -- ) "Print NOS as unsigned number right-justified with TOS width"
0024B2r 3               ; ## "u.r"  tested  ANS core ext
0024B2r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/UDotR"""
0024B2r 3               xt_u_dot_r:
0024B2r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
0024B5r 3               
0024B5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_to_r
0024B8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_zero
0024BBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_less_number_sign
0024BEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_number_sign_s
0024C1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_number_sign_greater
0024C4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_r_from
0024C7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over
0024CAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_minus
0024CDr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_spaces
0024D0r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_type
0024D3r 3               
0024D3r 3  60           z_u_dot_r:      rts
0024D4r 3               
0024D4r 3               
0024D4r 3               ; ## U_GREATER_THAN ( n m -- f ) "Return true if NOS > TOS (unsigned)"
0024D4r 3               ; ## "u>"  auto  ANS core ext
0024D4r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/Umore"""
0024D4r 3               xt_u_greater_than:
0024D4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
0024D7r 3               
0024D7r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0024D9r 3  D5 02                        cmp 2,x
0024DBr 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
0024DDr 3  F5 03                        sbc 3,x
0024DFr 3  E8                           inx
0024E0r 3  E8                           inx
0024E1r 3               
0024E1r 3  A9 00                        lda #0
0024E3r 3  69 FF                        adc #$ff
0024E5r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x         ; store flag
0024E7r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0024E9r 3               
0024E9r 3  60           z_u_greater_than:    rts
0024EAr 3               
0024EAr 3               ; ## U_LESS_THAN ( n m -- f ) "Return true if NOS < TOS (unsigned)"
0024EAr 3               ; ## "u<"  auto  ANS core
0024EAr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/Uless"""
0024EAr 3               xt_u_less_than:
0024EAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
0024EDr 3               
0024EDr 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
0024EFr 3  D5 00                        cmp 0,x
0024F1r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
0024F3r 3  F5 01                        sbc 1,x
0024F5r 3  E8                           inx
0024F6r 3  E8                           inx
0024F7r 3               
0024F7r 3  A9 00                        lda #0
0024F9r 3  69 FF                        adc #$ff
0024FBr 3  95 00                        sta 0,x         ; store flag
0024FDr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0024FFr 3               
0024FFr 3  60           z_u_less_than:    rts

002500r 3                       ; """
002500r 3               xt_ud_dot:

002503r 3

002513r 3

002513r 3               ; ## UD_DOT_R ( d u -- ) "Print unsigned double right-justified u wide"
002513r 3               ; ## "ud.r"  auto  Tali double
002513r 3                       ;

002513r 3               xt_ud_dot_r:

00251Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_number_sign_s
