002532r 3               
002532r 3               
002532r 3               ; ## UM_SLASH_MOD ( ud u -- ur u ) "32/16 -> 16 division"
002532r 3               ; ## "um/mod"  auto  ANS core
002532r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/UMDivMOD
002532r 3                       ; Divide double cell number by single cell number, returning the
002532r 3                       ; quotient as TOS and any remainder as NOS. All numbers are unsigned.
002532r 3                       ; This is the basic division operation all others use. Based on FIG
002532r 3                       ; Forth code, modified by Garth Wilson, see
002532r 3                       ; http://6502.org/source/integers/ummodfix/ummodfix.htm
002532r 3                       ;
002532r 3                       ; This uses tmp1, tmp1+1, and tmptos
002532r 3                       ; """
002532r 3               xt_um_slash_mod:
002532r 3               .scope
002532r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_3
002535r 3               
002535r 3                               ; catch division by zero
002535r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
002537r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
002539r 3  D0 05                        bne _not_zero
00253Br 3               
00253Br 3  A9 04                        lda #err_divzero
00253Dr 3  4C rr rr                     jmp error
002540r 3               
002540r 3               _not_zero:
002540r 3                               ; We loop 17 times
002540r 3  A9 11                        lda #17
002542r 3  85 2C                        sta tmptos
002544r 3               
002544r 3               _loop:
002544r 3                               ; rotate low cell of dividend one bit left (LSB)
002544r 3  36 04                        rol 4,x
002546r 3  36 05                        rol 5,x
002548r 3               
002548r 3                               ; loop control
002548r 3  C6 2C                        dec tmptos
00254Ar 3  F0 22                        beq _done
00254Cr 3               
00254Cr 3                               ; rotate high cell of dividend one bit left (MSB)
00254Cr 3  36 02                        rol 2,x
00254Er 3  36 03                        rol 3,x
002550r 3               
002550r 3  64 24                        stz tmp1        ; store the bit we got from hi cell (MSB)
002552r 3  26 24                        rol tmp1
002554r 3               
002554r 3                               ; subtract dividend hi cell minus divisor
002554r 3  38                           sec
002555r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
002557r 3  F5 00                        sbc 0,x
002559r 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
00255Br 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
00255Dr 3  F5 01                        sbc 1,x
00255Fr 3               
00255Fr 3  A8                           tay
002560r 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1
002562r 3  E9 00                        sbc #0
002564r 3  90 DE                        bcc _loop
002566r 3               
002566r 3                               ; make result new dividend high cell
002566r 3  A5 25                        lda tmp1+1
002568r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
00256Ar 3  94 03                        sty 3,x         ; used as temp storage
00256Cr 3               
00256Cr 3  80 D6                        bra _loop
00256Er 3               _done:
00256Er 3  E8                           inx
00256Fr 3  E8                           inx
002570r 3               
002570r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap
002573r 3               
002573r 3               .endscope
002573r 3  60           z_um_slash_mod: rts
002574r 3               
002574r 3               
002574r 3               ; ## UM_STAR ( u u -- ud ) "Multiply 16 x 16 -> 32"
002574r 3               ; ## "um*"  auto  ANS core
002574r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/UMTimes
002574r 3                       ; Multiply two unsigned 16 bit numbers, producing a 32 bit result.
002574r 3                       ; Old Forth versions such as FIG Forth call this U*
002574r 3                       ;
002574r 3                       ; This is based on modified FIG Forth code by Dr. Jefyll, see
002574r 3                       ; http://forum.6502.org/viewtopic.php?f=9&t=689 for a detailed
002574r 3                       ; discussion.
002574r 3                       ;
002574r 3                       ; We don't use the system scratch pad (SYSPAD) for temp
002574r 3                       ; storage because >NUMBER uses it as well, but instead tmp1 to
002574r 3                       ; tmp3 (tmp1 is N in the original code, tmp1+1 is N+1, etc).
002574r 3                       ;
002574r 3                       ; Consider switching to a table-supported version based on
002574r 3                       ; http://codebase64.org/doku.php?id=base:seriously_fast_multiplication
002574r 3                       ; http://codebase64.org/doku.php?id=magazines:chacking16#d_graphics_for_the_masseslib3d>
002574r 3                       ; http://forum.6502.org/viewtopic.php?p=205#p205
002574r 3                       ; http://forum.6502.org/viewtopic.php?f=9&t=689
002574r 3                       ; """
002574r 3               xt_um_star:
002574r 3               .scope
002574r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
002577r 3               
002577r 3                               ; to eliminate clc inside the loop, the value at
002577r 3                               ; tmp1 is reduced by 1 in advance
002577r 3  18                           clc
002578r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x         ; copy TOS to tmp2
00257Ar 3  E9 00                        sbc #0
00257Cr 3  85 26                        sta tmp2
00257Er 3               
00257Er 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
002580r 3  E9 00                        sbc #0
002582r 3  90 31                        bcc _zero       ; is TOS zero?
002584r 3  85 27                        sta tmp2+1
002586r 3               
002586r 3  A9 00                        lda #0
002588r 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
00258Ar 3  86 28                        stx tmp3        ; tested for exit from outer loop
00258Cr 3  CA                           dex
00258Dr 3  CA                           dex
00258Er 3               
00258Er 3               _outer_loop:
00258Er 3  A0 08                        ldy #8          ; counter inner loop
002590r 3  56 04                        lsr 4,x         ; think "2,x" then later "3,x"
002592r 3               
002592r 3               _inner_loop:
002592r 3  90 0C                        bcc _no_add
002594r 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1      ; save time, don't CLC
002596r 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1
002598r 3  65 26                        adc tmp2
00259Ar 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
00259Cr 3  A5 25                        lda tmp1+1
00259Er 3  65 27                        adc tmp2+1
0025A0r 3               
0025A0r 3               _no_add:
0025A0r 3  6A                           ror
0025A1r 3  66 24                        ror tmp1
0025A3r 3  76 04                        ror 4,x         ; think "2,x" then later "3,x"
0025A5r 3               
0025A5r 3  88                           dey
0025A6r 3  D0 EA                        bne _inner_loop ; go back for one more shift?
0025A8r 3               
0025A8r 3  E8                           inx
0025A9r 3  E4 28                        cpx tmp3
0025ABr 3  D0 E1                        bne _outer_loop ; go back for eight more shifts?
0025ADr 3               
0025ADr 3                               ; all done, store high word of result
0025ADr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0025AFr 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1
0025B1r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0025B3r 3  80 04                        bra _done
0025B5r 3               
0025B5r 3               _zero:
0025B5r 3  74 02                        stz 2,x
0025B7r 3  74 03                        stz 3,x
0025B9r 3               _done:
0025B9r 3               .endscope
0025B9r 3  60           z_um_star:      rts
0025BAr 3               
0025BAr 3               
0025BAr 3               ; ## UNLOOP ( -- )(R: n1 n2 n3 ---) "Drop loop control from Return stack"
0025BAr 3               ; ## "unloop"  auto  ANS core
0025BAr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/UNLOOP
0025BAr 3                       ;
0025BAr 3                       ; Note that 6xPLA uses just as many bytes as a loop would
0025BAr 3                       ; """
0025BAr 3               xt_unloop:
0025BAr 3                               ; Drop fudge number (limit/start from DO/?DO off the
0025BAr 3                               ; return stack
0025BAr 3  68                           pla
0025BBr 3  68                           pla
0025BCr 3  68                           pla
0025BDr 3  68                           pla
0025BEr 3               
0025BEr 3                               ; Now drop the LEAVE address that was below them off
0025BEr 3                               ; the Return Stack as well
0025BEr 3  68                           pla
0025BFr 3  68                           pla
0025C0r 3               
0025C0r 3  60           z_unloop:       rts
0025C1r 3               
0025C1r 3               
0025C1r 3               ; ## UNTIL (C: dest -- ) ( -- ) "Loop flow control"
0025C1r 3               ; ## "until"  auto  ANS core
0025C1r 3                       ; """http://forth-standard.org/standard/core/UNTIL"""
0025C1r 3               xt_until:
0025C1r 3                               ; Compile a 0BRANCH
0025C1r 3  A0 rr                        ldy #>zero_branch_runtime
0025C3r 3  A9 rr                        lda #<zero_branch_runtime
0025C5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_subroutine
0025C8r 3               
0025C8r 3                               ; The address to loop back to is on the stack.
0025C8r 3                               ; Just compile it as the destination for the
0025C8r 3                               ; 0branch.
0025C8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_comma
0025CBr 3               
0025CBr 3  60           z_until:        rts
0025CCr 3               
0025CCr 3               
0025CCr 3               ; ## UNUSED ( -- u ) "Return size of space available to Dictionary"
0025CCr 3               ; ## "unused"  auto  ANS core ext
0025CCr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/UNUSED
0025CCr 3                       ; UNUSED does not include the ACCEPT history buffers. Total RAM
0025CCr 3                       ; should be HERE + UNUSED + <history buffer size>, the last of which
0025CCr 3                       ; defaults to $400
0025CCr 3                       ; """
0025CCr 3               xt_unused:
0025CCr 3  CA                           dex
0025CDr 3  CA                           dex
0025CEr 3               
0025CEr 3  A9 00                        lda #<cp_end
0025D0r 3  38                           sec
0025D1r 3  E5 00                        sbc cp
0025D3r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0025D5r 3               
0025D5r 3  A9 7C                        lda #>cp_end
0025D7r 3  E5 01                        sbc cp+1
0025D9r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0025DBr 3               
0025DBr 3  60           z_unused:       rts
0025DCr 3               
0025DCr 3               
0025DCr 3               ; ## UPDATE ( -- ) "Mark current block as dirty"
0025DCr 3               ; ## "update"  auto  ANS block
0025DCr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/block/UPDATE"""
0025DCr 3               xt_update:
0025DCr 3                               ; Turn on the dirty bit. We can't use TSB here because it only
0025DCr 3                               ; has Absolute and Direct Pages addressing modes
0025DCr 3  A0 2C                        ldy #buffstatus_offset
0025DEr 3  B1 1E                        lda (up),y
0025E0r 3  09 02                        ora #2          ; Turn on dirty flag (bit 2)
0025E2r 3  91 1E                        sta (up),y
0025E4r 3               
0025E4r 3  60           z_update:       rts
0025E5r 3               
0025E5r 3               
0025E5r 3               ; ## USERADDR ( -- addr ) "Push address of base address of user variables"
0025E5r 3               ; ## "useraddr"  tested  Tali Forth
0025E5r 3               xt_useraddr:
0025E5r 3  CA                           dex
0025E6r 3  CA                           dex
0025E7r 3  A9 1E                        lda #<up
0025E9r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0025EBr 3  A9 00                        lda #>up
0025EDr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0025EFr 3               
0025EFr 3  60           z_useraddr:     rts
0025F0r 3               
0025F0r 3               
0025F0r 3               ; ## VALUE ( n "name" -- ) "Define a value"
0025F0r 3               ; ## "value"  auto  ANS core
0025F0r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/VALUE
0025F0r 3                       ;
0025F0r 3                       ; This is a dummy header for the WORDLIST. The actual code is
0025F0r 3                       ; identical to that of CONSTANT
0025F0r 3                       ; """
0025F0r 3               
0025F0r 3               
0025F0r 3               ; ## VARIABLE ( "name" -- ) "Define a variable"
0025F0r 3               ; ## "variable"  auto  ANS core
0025F0r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/VARIABLE
0025F0r 3                       ; There are various Forth definitions for this word, such as
0025F0r 3                       ; `CREATE 1 CELLS ALLOT`  or  `CREATE 0 ,`  We use a variant of the
0025F0r 3                       ; second one so the variable is initialized to zero
0025F0r 3                       ; """
0025F0r 3               xt_variable:
0025F0r 3                               ; we let CREATE do the heavy lifting
0025F0r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_create
0025F3r 3               
0025F3r 3                               ; there is no "STZ (CP)" so we have to do this the hard
0025F3r 3                               ; way
0025F3r 3  A9 00                        lda #0
0025F5r 3               
0025F5r 3  92 00                        sta (cp)
0025F7r 3  E6 00                        inc cp
0025F9r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
0025FBr 3  E6 01                        inc cp+1
0025FDr 3               :
0025FDr 3  92 00                        sta (cp)
0025FFr 3  E6 00                        inc cp
002601r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
002603r 3  E6 01                        inc cp+1
002605r 3               :
002605r 3                               ; Now we need to adjust the length of the complete word by two
002605r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr adjust_z
002608r 3               
002608r 3  60           z_variable:     rts
002609r 3               
002609r 3               
002609r 3               ; ## WHILE ( C: dest -- orig dest ) ( x -- ) "Loop flow control"
002609r 3               ; ## "while"  auto  ANS core
002609r 3                       ; """http://forth-standard.org/standard/core/WHILE"""
002609r 3               xt_while:
002609r 3                               ; Compile a 0branch
002609r 3  A0 rr                        ldy #>zero_branch_runtime
00260Br 3  A9 rr                        lda #<zero_branch_runtime
00260Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_subroutine
002610r 3               
002610r 3                               ; Put the address (here) where the destination
002610r 3                               ; address needs to go so it can be put there later.
002610r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_here
002613r 3               
002613r 3                               ; Fill in the destination address with 0 for now.
002613r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_zero
002616r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_comma
002619r 3               
002619r 3                               ; Swap the two addresses on the stack.
002619r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap
00261Cr 3               
00261Cr 3  60           z_while:        rts
00261Dr 3               
00261Dr 3               
00261Dr 3               ; ## WITHIN ( n1 n2 n3 -- ) "See if within a range"
00261Dr 3               ; ## "within"  auto  ANS core ext
00261Dr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/WITHIN
00261Dr 3                       ;
00261Dr 3                       ; This an assembler version of the ANS Forth implementation
00261Dr 3                       ; at https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/WITHIN which is
00261Dr 3                       ; OVER - >R - R> U<  note there is an alternative high-level version
00261Dr 3                       ; ROT TUCK > -ROT > INVERT AND
00261Dr 3                       ; """"
00261Dr 3               xt_within:
00261Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_3
002620r 3               
002620r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over
002623r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_minus
002626r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_to_r
002629r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_minus
00262Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_r_from
00262Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_less_than
002632r 3               
002632r 3  60           z_within:       rts
002633r 3               
002633r 3               
002633r 3               ; ## WORD ( char "name " -- caddr ) "Parse input stream"
002633r 3               ; ## "word"  auto  ANS core
002633r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/WORD
002633r 3                       ; Obsolete parsing word included for backwards compatibility only.
002633r 3                       ; Do not use this, use `PARSE` or `PARSE-NAME`. Skips leading delimiters
002633r 3                       ; and copies word to storage area for a maximum size of 255 bytes.
002633r 3                       ; Returns the result as a counted string (requires COUNT to convert
002633r 3                       ; to modern format), and inserts a space after the string. See "Forth
002633r 3                       ; Programmer's Handbook" 3rd edition p. 159 and
002633r 3                       ; http://www.forth200x.org/documents/html/rationale.html#rat:core:PARSE
002633r 3                       ; for discussions of why you shouldn't be using WORD anymore.
002633r 3                       ;
002633r 3                       ; Forth
002633r 3                       ; would be   PARSE DUP BUFFER1 C! OUTPUT 1+ SWAP MOVE BUFFER1
002633r 3                       ; We only allow input of 255 chars. Seriously, use PARSE-NAME.
002633r 3                       ; """
002633r 3               xt_word:
002633r 3               .scope
002633r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
002636r 3               
002636r 3                               ; Skip over leading delimiters - this is like PARSE-NAME,
002636r 3                               ; but unlike PARSE
002636r 3  A4 0C                        ldy toin                ; >IN
002638r 3               _loop:
002638r 3  C4 0A                        cpy ciblen              ; quit if end of input
00263Ar 3  F0 09                        beq _found_char
00263Cr 3  B1 08                        lda (cib),y
00263Er 3  D5 00                        cmp 0,x                 ; ASCII of delimiter
002640r 3  D0 03                        bne _found_char
002642r 3               
002642r 3  C8                           iny
002643r 3  80 F3                        bra _loop
002645r 3               _found_char:
002645r 3                               ; Save index of where word starts
002645r 3  84 0C                        sty toin
002647r 3               
002647r 3                               ; The real work is done by parse
002647r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_parse            ; Returns ( addr u )
00264Ar 3               
00264Ar 3                               ; Convert the modern ( addr u ) string format to obsolete
00264Ar 3                               ; ( caddr ) format. We just do this in the Dictionary
00264Ar 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00264Cr 3  92 00                        sta (cp)                ; Save length of string
00264Er 3  48                           pha                     ; Keep copy of length for later
00264Fr 3               
00264Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; ( addr u u )
002652r 3  A5 00                        lda cp
002654r 3  18                           clc
002655r 3  69 01                        adc #1
002657r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x                 ; LSB of CP
002659r 3  A5 01                        lda cp+1
00265Br 3  69 00                        adc #0
00265Dr 3  95 03                        sta 3,x                 ; ( addr cp+1 u )
00265Fr 3               
00265Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_move
002662r 3               
002662r 3                               ; Return caddr
002662r 3  CA                           dex
002663r 3  CA                           dex
002664r 3  A5 00                        lda cp
002666r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002668r 3  A5 01                        lda cp+1
00266Ar 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00266Cr 3               
00266Cr 3                               ; Adjust CP
00266Cr 3  68                           pla                     ; length of string
00266Dr 3  18                           clc
00266Er 3  65 00                        adc cp
002670r 3  85 00                        sta cp
002672r 3  A5 01                        lda cp+1
002674r 3  69 00                        adc #0                  ; we only need the carry
002676r 3  85 01                        sta cp+1
002678r 3               .endscope
002678r 3  60           z_word:         rts
002679r 3               
002679r 3               ; ## WORDLIST ( -- wid ) "Create new wordlist (from pool of 8)"
002679r 3               ; ## "wordlist" auto ANS search
002679r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/search/WORDLIST
002679r 3                       ; See the tutorial on Wordlists and the Search Order for
002679r 3                       ; more information.
002679r 3                       ; """
002679r 3               xt_wordlist:
002679r 3               .scope
002679r 3                               ; Get the current number of wordlists
002679r 3  A0 05                        ldy #num_wordlists_offset
00267Br 3  B1 1E                        lda (up),y      ; This is a byte variable, so only
00267Dr 3                                               ; the LSB needs to be checked.
00267Dr 3               
00267Dr 3                               ; See if we are already at the max.
00267Dr 3  C9 0C                        cmp #max_wordlists
00267Fr 3  D0 05                        bne _ok
002681r 3               
002681r 3                               ; Print an error message if all wordlists used.
002681r 3  A9 0B                        lda #err_wordlist
002683r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp error
002686r 3               
002686r 3               _ok:
002686r 3  1A                           inc             ; Increment the wordlist#
002687r 3  91 1E                        sta (up),y      ; Save it into byte variable #wordlists
002689r 3  CA                           dex             ; and put it on the stack.
00268Ar 3  CA                           dex
00268Br 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00268Dr 3  74 01                        stz 1,x         ; 12 is the max, so upper byte is always zero.
00268Fr 3               
00268Fr 3               .endscope
00268Fr 3  60           z_wordlist:     rts
002690r 3               
002690r 3               
002690r 3               ; ## WORDS ( -- ) "Print known words from Dictionary"
002690r 3               ; ## "words"  tested  ANS tools
002690r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/tools/WORDS
002690r 3                       ; This is pretty much only used at the command line so we can
002690r 3                       ; be slow and try to save space.
002690r 3                       ; """
002690r 3               xt_words:
002690r 3               .scope
002690r 3                               ; we follow Gforth by starting on the next
002690r 3                               ; line
002690r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
002693r 3               
002693r 3                               ; We pretty-format the output by inserting a line break
002693r 3                               ; before the end of the line. We can get away with pushing
002693r 3                               ; the counter to the stack because this is usually an
002693r 3                               ; interactive word and speed is not that important
002693r 3  A9 00                        lda #0
002695r 3  48                           pha
002696r 3               
002696r 3                               ; Set up for traversing the wordlist search order.
002696r 3  CA                           dex                     ; Make room on the stack for
002697r 3  CA                           dex                     ; a dictionary pointer.
002698r 3  64 28                        stz tmp3                ; Start at the beginning of
00269Ar 3                                                       ; the search order.
00269Ar 3               _wordlist_loop:
00269Ar 3  A0 1E                        ldy #num_order_offset   ; Check against byte variable #ORDER.
00269Cr 3  A5 28                        lda tmp3
00269Er 3  D1 1E                        cmp (up),y              ; See if we are done.
0026A0r 3  D0 02                        bne _have_wordlist
0026A2r 3               
0026A2r 3                               ; We ran out of wordlists to search.
0026A2r 3  80 44                        bra _words_done
0026A4r 3               
0026A4r 3               _have_wordlist:
0026A4r 3                               ; start with last word in Dictionary
0026A4r 3                               ; Get the current wordlist id
0026A4r 3  18                           clc                     ; Index into byte array SEARCH-ORDER.
0026A5r 3  69 1F                        adc #search_order_offset
0026A7r 3  A8                           tay
0026A8r 3  B1 1E                        lda (up),y              ; Get the index into array WORDLISTS
0026AAr 3               
0026AAr 3                               ; Get the DP for that wordlist.
0026AAr 3  0A                           asl                     ; Turn offset into cells offset.
0026ABr 3  18                           clc
0026ACr 3  69 06                        adc #wordlists_offset
0026AEr 3  A8                           tay
0026AFr 3  B1 1E                        lda (up),y              ; Save the DP for this wordlist
0026B1r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x                 ; on the stack. ( nt )
0026B3r 3  C8                           iny
0026B4r 3  B1 1E                        lda (up),y
0026B6r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0026B8r 3               
0026B8r 3               _loop:
0026B8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; ( nt nt )
0026BBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_name_to_string   ; ( nt addr u )
0026BEr 3               
0026BEr 3                               ; Insert line break if we're about to go past the end of the
0026BEr 3                               ; line
0026BEr 3  68                           pla
0026BFr 3  18                           clc
0026C0r 3  75 00                        adc 0,x
0026C2r 3  1A                           inc                     ; don't forget the space between words
0026C3r 3  C9 4F                        cmp #MAX_LINE_LENGTH    ; usually 79
0026C5r 3  90 05                        bcc :+
0026C7r 3               
0026C7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
0026CAr 3  A9 00                        lda #0
0026CCr 3               :
0026CCr 3  48                           pha
0026CDr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_type             ; ( nt )
0026D0r 3               
0026D0r 3  A9 20                        lda #AscSP
0026D2r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr emit_a
0026D5r 3               
0026D5r 3                               ; get next word, which begins two down
0026D5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+
0026D8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+
0026DBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; @ ( nt+1 )
0026DEr 3               
0026DEr 3                               ; if next address is zero, we're done
0026DEr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0026E0r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
0026E2r 3  D0 D4                        bne _loop
0026E4r 3               
0026E4r 3                               ; Move on to the next wordlist in the search order.
0026E4r 3  E6 28                        inc tmp3
0026E6r 3  80 B2                        bra _wordlist_loop
0026E8r 3               
0026E8r 3               _words_done:
0026E8r 3  68                           pla                     ; dump counter
0026E9r 3               
0026E9r 3  E8                           inx
0026EAr 3  E8                           inx
0026EBr 3               
0026EBr 3               .endscope
0026EBr 3  60           z_words:        rts
0026ECr 3               
0026ECr 3               
0026ECr 3               ; ## WORDSIZE ( nt -- u ) "Get size of word in bytes"
0026ECr 3               ; ## "wordsize"  auto  Tali Forth
0026ECr 3                       ; """Given an word's name token (nt), return the size of the
0026ECr 3                       ; word's payload size in bytes (CFA plus PFA) in bytes. Does not
0026ECr 3                       ; count the final RTS.
0026ECr 3                       ; """
0026ECr 3               xt_wordsize:
0026ECr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
0026EFr 3               
0026EFr 3                               ; We get the start address of the word from its header entry
0026EFr 3                               ; for the start of the actual code (execution token, xt)
0026EFr 3                               ; which is four bytes down, and the pointer to the end of the
0026EFr 3                               ; code (z_word, six bytes down)
0026EFr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0026F1r 3  85 24                        sta tmp1
0026F3r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
0026F5r 3  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
0026F7r 3               
0026F7r 3  A0 06                        ldy #6
0026F9r 3  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y    ; LSB of z
0026FBr 3  88                           dey
0026FCr 3  88                           dey
0026FDr 3               
0026FDr 3  38                           sec
0026FEr 3  F1 24                        sbc (tmp1),y    ; LSB of xt
002700r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002702r 3               
002702r 3  A0 07                        ldy #7
002704r 3  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y    ; MSB of z
002706r 3  88                           dey
002707r 3  88                           dey
002708r 3               
002708r 3  F1 24                        sbc (tmp1),y    ; MSB of xt
00270Ar 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00270Cr 3               
00270Cr 3  60           z_wordsize:     rts
00270Dr 3               
00270Dr 3               
00270Dr 3               ; ## XOR ( n n -- n ) "Logically XOR TOS and NOS"
00270Dr 3               ; ## "xor"  auto  ANS core
00270Dr 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/XOR"""
00270Dr 3               xt_xor:
00270Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_2
002710r 3               
002710r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
002712r 3  55 02                        eor 2,x
002714r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
002716r 3               
002716r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
002718r 3  55 03                        eor 3,x
00271Ar 3  95 03                        sta 3,x
00271Cr 3               
00271Cr 3  E8                           inx
00271Dr 3  E8                           inx
00271Er 3               
00271Er 3  60           z_xor:          rts
00271Fr 3               
00271Fr 3               
00271Fr 3               ; ## ZERO ( -- 0 ) "Push 0 to Data Stack"
00271Fr 3               ; ## "0"  auto  Tali Forth
00271Fr 3                       ; """The disassembler assumes that this routine does not use Y. Note
00271Fr 3                       ; that CASE and FORTH-WORDLIST use the same routine, as the WD for Forth
00271Fr 3                       ; is 0."""
00271Fr 3               xt_case:
00271Fr 3               xt_forth_wordlist:
00271Fr 3               xt_zero:
00271Fr 3  CA                           dex             ; push
002720r 3  CA                           dex
002721r 3  74 00                        stz 0,x
002723r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
002725r 3               z_case:
002725r 3               z_forth_wordlist:
002725r 3               z_zero:
002725r 3  60                           rts
002726r 3               
002726r 3               
002726r 3               ; ## ZERO_EQUAL ( n -- f ) "Check if TOS is zero"
002726r 3               ; ## "0="  auto  ANS core
002726r 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/ZeroEqual"""
002726r 3               xt_zero_equal:
002726r 3               .scope
002726r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
002729r 3               
002729r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00272Br 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00272Dr 3  F0 04                        beq _zero
00272Fr 3               
00272Fr 3                               ; not a zero, so we need a FALSE flag (0)
00272Fr 3  A9 00                        lda #0
002731r 3  80 02                        bra _store
002733r 3               _zero:
002733r 3                               ; We have a zero, so we need a TRUE flag (-1)
002733r 3  A9 FF                        lda #$ff
002735r 3               _store:
002735r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002737r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
002739r 3               
002739r 3               .endscope
002739r 3  60           z_zero_equal:   rts
00273Ar 3               
00273Ar 3               
00273Ar 3               ; ## ZERO_GREATER ( n -- f ) "Return a TRUE flag if TOS is positive"
00273Ar 3               ; ## "0>"  auto  ANS core ext
00273Ar 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/Zeromore"""
00273Ar 3               xt_zero_greater:
00273Ar 3               .scope
00273Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
00273Dr 3               
00273Dr 3  A0 00                        ldy #0          ; Default is FALSE (TOS is negative)
00273Fr 3               
00273Fr 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x         ; MSB
002741r 3  30 05                        bmi _done       ; TOS is negative, keep FLASE
002743r 3  15 00                        ora 0,x
002745r 3  F0 01                        beq _done       ; TOS is zero, keep FALSE
002747r 3               
002747r 3  88                           dey             ; TOS is postive, make true
002748r 3               _done:
002748r 3  98                           tya
002749r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00274Br 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00274Dr 3               
00274Dr 3               .endscope
00274Dr 3  60           z_zero_greater: rts
00274Er 3               
00274Er 3               
00274Er 3               ; ## ZERO_LESS ( n -- f ) "Return a TRUE flag if TOS negative"
00274Er 3               ; ## "0<"  auto  ANS core
00274Er 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/Zeroless"""
00274Er 3               xt_zero_less:
00274Er 3               .scope
00274Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
002751r 3               
002751r 3  A0 00                        ldy #0          ; Default is FALSE (TOS positive)
002753r 3               
002753r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x         ; MSB
002755r 3  10 01                        bpl _done       ; TOS is positive, so keep FALSE
002757r 3               
002757r 3  88                           dey             ; TOS is negative, make TRUE
002758r 3               _done:
002758r 3  98                           tya
002759r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00275Br 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00275Dr 3               
00275Dr 3               .endscope
00275Dr 3  60           z_zero_less:    rts
00275Er 3               
00275Er 3               
00275Er 3               ; ## ZERO_UNEQUAL ( m -- f ) "Return TRUE flag if not zero"
00275Er 3               ; ## "0<>"  auto  ANS core ext
00275Er 3                       ; """https://forth-standard.org/standard/core/Zerone"""
00275Er 3               xt_zero_unequal:
00275Er 3               .scope
00275Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
002761r 3               
002761r 3  A0 00                        ldy #0          ; default false
002763r 3               
002763r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
002765r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
002767r 3  F0 01                        beq _got_zero
002769r 3               
002769r 3  88                           dey
00276Ar 3               _got_zero:
00276Ar 3  98                           tya
00276Br 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00276Dr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00276Fr 3               
00276Fr 3               .endscope
00276Fr 3  60           z_zero_unequal: rts
002770r 3               
002770r 3               
002770r 3               ; ==========================================================
002770r 3               ; EDITOR words
002770r 3               
002770r 3               ; This routine is used by both enter-screen and erase-screen
002770r 3               ; to get a buffer for the given screen number and set SCR to
002770r 3               ; the given screen number.  This word is not in the dictionary.
002770r 3               xt_editor_screen_helper:
002770r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup
002773r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_scr
002776r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_store
002779r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_buffer
00277Cr 3               z_editor_screen_helper:
00277Cr 3  60                           rts
00277Dr 3               
00277Dr 3               
00277Dr 3               ; ## EDITOR_ENTER_SCREEN ( scr# -- ) "Enter all lines for given screen"
00277Dr 3               ; ## "enter-screen"  auto  Tali Editor
00277Dr 3               xt_editor_enter_screen:
00277Dr 3               .scope
00277Dr 3                               ; Set the variable SCR and get a buffer for the
00277Dr 3                               ; given screen number.
00277Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_editor_screen_helper
002780r 3               
002780r 3                               ; Drop the buffer address.
002780r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_drop
002783r 3               
002783r 3                               ; Overwrite the lines one at a time.
002783r 3  64 2E                        stz editor1
002785r 3               _prompt_loop:
002785r 3                               ; Put the current line number on the stack.
002785r 3  CA                           dex
002786r 3  CA                           dex
002787r 3  A5 2E                        lda editor1
002789r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00278Br 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
00278Dr 3               
00278Dr 3                               ; Use the O word to prompt for overwrite.
00278Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_editor_o
002790r 3               
002790r 3                               ; Move on to the next line.
002790r 3  E6 2E                        inc editor1
002792r 3  A9 10                        lda #16
002794r 3  C5 2E                        cmp editor1
002796r 3  D0 ED                        bne _prompt_loop
002798r 3               
002798r 3               .endscope
002798r 3               z_editor_enter_screen:
002798r 3  60                           rts
002799r 3               
002799r 3               
002799r 3               ; ## EDITOR_ERASE_SCREEN ( scr# -- ) "Erase all lines for given screen"
002799r 3               ; ## "erase-screen"  tested  Tali Editor
002799r 3               xt_editor_erase_screen:
002799r 3                               ; Set the variable SCR and get a buffer for the
002799r 3                               ; given screen number.
002799r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_editor_screen_helper
00279Cr 3               
00279Cr 3                               ; Put 1024 (chars/screen) on stack.
00279Cr 3  CA                           dex
00279Dr 3  CA                           dex
00279Er 3  74 00                        stz 0,x
0027A0r 3  A9 04                        lda #4          ; 4 in MSB makes 1024 ($400).
0027A2r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0027A4r 3               
0027A4r 3                               ; Erase the entire block (fill with spaces).
0027A4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_blank
0027A7r 3               
0027A7r 3                               ; Mark buffer as updated.
0027A7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_update
0027AAr 3               
0027AAr 3               z_editor_erase_screen:
0027AAr 3  60                           rts
0027ABr 3               
0027ABr 3               
0027ABr 3               ; ## EDITOR_EL ( line# -- ) "Erase the given line number"
0027ABr 3               ; ## "el"  tested  Tali Editor
0027ABr 3               xt_editor_el:
0027ABr 3                               ; Turn the line number into buffer offset.
0027ABr 3                               ; This also loads the block into the buffer if it's
0027ABr 3                               ; not there for some reason.
0027ABr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_editor_line
0027AEr 3               
0027AEr 3                               ; Put 64 (# of chars/line) on the stack.
0027AEr 3  CA                           dex
0027AFr 3  CA                           dex
0027B0r 3  A9 40                        lda #64
0027B2r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0027B4r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
0027B6r 3               
0027B6r 3                               ; Fill with spaces.
0027B6r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_blank
0027B9r 3               
0027B9r 3                               ; Mark buffer as updated.
0027B9r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_update
0027BCr 3               
0027BCr 3  60           z_editor_el:    rts
0027BDr 3               
0027BDr 3               
0027BDr 3               ; ## EDITOR_L ( -- ) "List the current screen"
0027BDr 3               ; ## "l"  tested  Tali Editor
0027BDr 3               xt_editor_l:
0027BDr 3               .scope
0027BDr 3                               ; Load the current screen
0027BDr 3  CA                           dex             ; Put SCR on the stack.
0027BEr 3  CA                           dex
0027BFr 3  A0 02                        ldy #scr_offset
0027C1r 3  B1 1E                        lda (up),y
0027C3r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0027C5r 3  C8                           iny
0027C6r 3  B1 1E                        lda (up),y
0027C8r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0027CAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_block    ; Get the current screen.
0027CDr 3               
0027CDr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
0027D0r 3               
0027D0r 3                               ; Print the screen number.
0027D0r 3                               ; We're using sliteral, so we need to set up the
0027D0r 3                               ; appropriate data structure (see sliteral)
0027D0r 3  80 08                        bra _after_screen_msg
0027D2r 3               
0027D2r 3               _screen_msg:
0027D2r 3  53 63 72 65                  .byte "Screen #"
0027D6r 3  65 6E 20 23  
0027DAr 3               
0027DAr 3               _after_screen_msg:
0027DAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr sliteral_runtime
0027DDr 3  rr rr 08 00                  .word _screen_msg, _after_screen_msg-_screen_msg
0027E1r 3               
0027E1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_type
0027E4r 3               
0027E4r 3                               ; Put the screen number and printed size for u.r on the stack.
0027E4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_scr
0027E7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch
0027EAr 3  CA                           dex
0027EBr 3  CA                           dex
0027ECr 3  A9 04                        lda #4          ; four spaces
0027EEr 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0027F0r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
0027F2r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot_r
0027F5r 3               
0027F5r 3                               ; The address of the buffer is currently on the stack.
0027F5r 3                               ; Print 64 chars at a time. TYPE uses tmp1, so we'll
0027F5r 3                               ; keep track of the line number in tmp3.
0027F5r 3  64 28                        stz tmp3
0027F7r 3               
0027F7r 3               _line_loop:
0027F7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
0027FAr 3               
0027FAr 3                               ; Print the line number (2-space fixed width)
0027FAr 3  CA                           dex
0027FBr 3  CA                           dex
0027FCr 3  CA                           dex
0027FDr 3  CA                           dex
0027FEr 3  74 03                        stz 3,x
002800r 3  A5 28                        lda tmp3
002802r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
002804r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
002806r 3  A9 02                        lda #2
002808r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00280Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot_r
00280Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_space
002810r 3               
002810r 3                               ; Print one line using the address on the stack.
002810r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup
002813r 3  CA                           dex
002814r 3  CA                           dex
002815r 3  A9 40                        lda #64
002817r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002819r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
00281Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_type
00281Er 3               
00281Er 3                               ; Add 64 to the address on the stack to move to the next line.
00281Er 3  18                           clc
00281Fr 3  A9 40                        lda #64
002821r 3  75 00                        adc 0,x
002823r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002825r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
002827r 3  69 00                        adc #0      ; Add carry
002829r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
00282Br 3               
00282Br 3                               ; Increment the line number (held in tmp3)
00282Br 3  E6 28                        inc tmp3
00282Dr 3               
00282Dr 3                               ; See if we are done.
00282Dr 3  A5 28                        lda tmp3
00282Fr 3  C9 10                        cmp #16
002831r 3  D0 C4                        bne _line_loop
002833r 3               
002833r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
002836r 3                               ; Drop the address on the stack.
002836r 3  E8                           inx
002837r 3  E8                           inx
002838r 3               
002838r 3               .endscope
002838r 3  60           z_editor_l:            rts
002839r 3               
002839r 3               
002839r 3               ; ## EDITOR_LINE ( line# -- c-addr ) "Turn a line number into address in current screen"
002839r 3               ; ## "line"  tested  Tali Editor
002839r 3               xt_editor_line:
002839r 3               .scope
002839r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr underflow_1
00283Cr 3               
00283Cr 3                               ; Multiply the TOS by 64 (chars/line) to compute offset.
00283Cr 3  A0 06                        ldy #6          ; *64 is same as left shift 6 times.
00283Er 3               _shift_tos_left:
00283Er 3  16 00                        asl 0,x         ; Shift TOS to the left
002840r 3  36 01                        rol 1,x         ; ROL brings MSb from lower byte.
002842r 3  88                           dey
002843r 3  D0 F9                        bne _shift_tos_left
002845r 3                               ; Load the current screen into a buffer
002845r 3                               ; and get the buffer address
002845r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_scr
002848r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch
00284Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_block
00284Er 3               
00284Er 3                               ; Add the offset to the buffer base address.
00284Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_plus
002851r 3               
002851r 3               .endscope
002851r 3  60           z_editor_line:  rts
002852r 3               
002852r 3               
002852r 3               ; ## EDITOR_O ( line# -- ) "Overwrite the given line"
002852r 3               ; ## "o"  tested  Tali Editor
002852r 3               xt_editor_o:
002852r 3                               ; Print prompt
002852r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
002855r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup
002858r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_two
00285Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot_r
00285Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_space
002861r 3  A9 2A                        lda #42         ; ASCII for *
002863r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr emit_a
002866r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_space
002869r 3               
002869r 3                               ; Accept new input (directly into the buffer)
002869r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_editor_line
00286Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup      ; Save a copy of the line address for later.
00286Fr 3  CA                           dex
002870r 3  CA                           dex
002871r 3  A9 40                        lda #64         ; chars/line
002873r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002875r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
002877r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_accept
00287Ar 3               
00287Ar 3                               ; Fill the rest with spaces.
00287Ar 3                               ; Stack is currently ( line_address numchars_from_accept )
00287Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup
00287Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_not_rote ; -rot
002880r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_plus
002883r 3  CA                           dex
002884r 3  CA                           dex
002885r 3  A9 40                        lda #64         ; chars/line
002887r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
002889r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x
00288Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_rot
00288Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_minus
002891r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_blank
002894r 3               
002894r 3                               ; Mark buffer as updated.
002894r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_update
002897r 3               
002897r 3  60           z_editor_o:     rts
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; END
002898r 3               
002898r 2               .include "assembler.asm"        ; SAN assembler
002898r 3               ; Assembler for Tali Forth 2
002898r 3               ; Scot W. Stevenson <scot.stevenson@gmail.com>
002898r 3               ; First version: 07. Nov 2014 (as tasm65c02)
002898r 3               ; This version: 03. Jan 2019
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; This is the built-in assembler for Tali Forth 2. Once the assembler wordlist
002898r 3               ; is included with
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ;       assembler-wordlist >order
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; the opcodes are available as normal Forth words. The format is Simpler
002898r 3               ; Assembler Notation (SAN) which separates the opcode completely from the
002898r 3               ; operand (see https://github.com/scotws/SAN). In this case, the operand is
002898r 3               ; entered before the opcode in the postfix Forth notation (for example, "2000
002898r 3               ; lda.#"). See the assembler documenation in the manual for more detail.
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; The code here was originally used in A Typist's Assembler for the 65c02
002898r 3               ; (tasm65c02), see https://github.com/scotws/tasm65c02 for the standalone
002898r 3               ; version. Tasm65c02 is in the public domain.
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; This code makes use of the opcode tables stored as part of the disassembler.
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; ==========================================================
002898r 3               ; MNEMONICS
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; The assembler instructions are realized as individual Forth words with
002898r 3               ; entries in the assembler wordlist (see header.asm). We pass the opcode in A.
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; An alterantive method would have been along the lines of
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ;               jsr asm_common
002898r 3               ;               .byte $EA
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; where the asm_common then uses the address on the Return Stack to pick up the
002898r 3               ; opcode and the length. Though this uses fewer resources, the current version
002898r 3               ; makes up for this by simplifying the code of asm_common.
002898r 3               
002898r 3               ; The routines are organized alphabetically by SAN mnemonic, not by opcode. The
002898r 3               ; SAN and traditional mnemonics are listed after the opcode load instruction.
002898r 3               ; This list was generated by a Python script in the tools folder, see there for
002898r 3               ; more detail.
002898r 3               
002898r 3               assembler:              ; used to calculate size of assembler code
002898r 3               
002898r 3               xt_asm_adc_h:   ; adc.# \ ADC #nn
002898r 3  A9 69                        lda #$69
00289Ar 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
00289Dr 3               z_asm_adc_h:
00289Dr 3               
00289Dr 3               xt_asm_adc_x:   ; adc.x \ ADC nnnn,X
00289Dr 3  A9 7D                        lda #$7D
00289Fr 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028A2r 3               z_asm_adc_x:
0028A2r 3               
0028A2r 3               xt_asm_adc_y:   ; adc.y \ ADC nnnn,Y
0028A2r 3  A9 79                        lda #$79
0028A4r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028A7r 3               z_asm_adc_y:
0028A7r 3               
0028A7r 3               xt_asm_adc_z:   ; adc.z \ ADC nn
0028A7r 3  A9 65                        lda #$65
0028A9r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028ACr 3               z_asm_adc_z:
0028ACr 3               
0028ACr 3               xt_asm_adc_zi:  ; adc.zi \ ADC (nn)
0028ACr 3  A9 72                        lda #$72
0028AEr 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028B1r 3               z_asm_adc_zi:
0028B1r 3               
0028B1r 3               xt_asm_adc_ziy: ; adc.ziy \ ADC (nn),Y
0028B1r 3  A9 71                        lda #$71
0028B3r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028B6r 3               z_asm_adc_ziy:
0028B6r 3               
0028B6r 3               xt_asm_adc_zx:  ; adc.zx \ ADC nn,X
0028B6r 3  A9 75                        lda #$75
0028B8r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028BBr 3               z_asm_adc_zx:
0028BBr 3               
0028BBr 3               xt_asm_adc_zxi: ; adc.zxi \ ADC (nn,X)
0028BBr 3  A9 61                        lda #$61
0028BDr 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028C0r 3               z_asm_adc_zxi:
0028C0r 3               
0028C0r 3               xt_asm_and:     ; and. \ AND nnnn
0028C0r 3  A9 2D                        lda #$2D
0028C2r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028C5r 3               z_asm_and:
0028C5r 3               
0028C5r 3               xt_asm_and_h:   ; and.# \ AND #nn
0028C5r 3  A9 29                        lda #$29
0028C7r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
0028CAr 3               z_asm_and_h:
0028CAr 3

002AA1r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
002AA4r 3               z_asm_lsr_a:

002AB0r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp asm_common
002AB3r 3               z_asm_lsr_zx:

002B77r 3               z_asm_sbc_zxi:

002B91r 3  80 60                        bra asm_common
002B93r 3               z_asm_sta_z:

002B95r 3  80 5C                        bra asm_common
002B97r 3               z_asm_sta_zi:
002B97r 3

002B97r 3  A9 91                        lda #$91
002B99r 3  80 58                        bra asm_common
002B9Br 3               z_asm_sta_ziy:

002B9Br 3  A9 95                        lda #$95

002B9Fr 3               z_asm_sta_zx:

002B9Fr 3  A9 81                        lda #$81
002BA1r 3  80 50                        bra asm_common
002BA3r 3               z_asm_sta_zxi:
002BA3r 3

002BA3r 3  A9 8E                        lda #$8E

002BA7r 3               z_asm_stx:

002BAFr 3               z_asm_stx_zy:

002BB7r 3               z_asm_sty_z:

002BD5r 3  80 1C                        bra asm_common
002BD7r 3               z_asm_trb:

002BDDr 3  80 14                        bra asm_common
002BDFr 3               z_asm_tsb:

002BE1r 3  80 10                        bra asm_common
002BE3r 3               z_asm_tsb_z:

002BE3r 3  A9 BA                        lda #$BA
002BE5r 3  80 0C                        bra asm_common
002BE7r 3               z_asm_tsx:
002BE7r 3
