0035ACr 3                               ; If there is only one character and that character is a
0035ACr 3                               ; dot, we're done with adding text and switch back to command
0035ACr 3                               ; mode
0035ACr 3  B2 08                        lda (cib)
0035AEr 3  C9 2E                        cmp #$2e                ; ASCII for '.'
0035B0r 3  D0 16                        bne _add_line
0035B2r 3               
0035B2r 3                               ; So it's a dot, but that the only character in the line?
0035B2r 3                               ; We want the length to be one character exactly
0035B2r 3  A4 0A                        ldy ciblen
0035B4r 3  C0 01                        cpy #01
0035B6r 3  D0 10                        bne _add_line
0035B8r 3               
0035B8r 3  A4 0B                        ldy ciblen+1
0035BAr 3  D0 0C                        bne _add_line
0035BCr 3               
0035BCr 3                               ; Yes, it is a dot, so we're done adding lines.
0035BCr 3  E8                           inx
0035BDr 3  E8                           inx
0035BEr 3               
0035BEr 3                               ; The string is stored and the new node is full. Time to set the
0035BEr 3                               ; changed flag
0035BEr 3  A9 40                        lda #%01000000
0035C0r 3  04 32                        tsb ed_flags
0035C2r 3               
0035C2r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
0035C5r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _input_loop
0035C8r 3               
0035C8r 3               _add_line:
0035C8r 3                               ; Break the linked list so we can insert another node
0035C8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-t u-t addr1 addr1 )
0035CBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_here             ; HERE ( addr-t u-t addr1 addr1 here )
0035CEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr-t u-t addr1 here addr1 )
0035D1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; @  ( addr-t u-t addr1 here addr2 )
0035D4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_comma            ; ,  ( addr-t u-t addr1 here )
0035D7r 3               
0035D7r 3                               ; We're going to need that HERE for the next line if more
0035D7r 3                               ; than one line is added. This is a good time to save it on
0035D7r 3                               ; the stack
0035D7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_tuck             ; TUCK ( addr-t u-t here addr1 here )
0035DAr 3               
0035DAr 3                               ; We have now saved the link to the next node at HERE, which is
0035DAr 3                               ; where the CP was pointing. CP has been advanced by one cell,
0035DAr 3                               ; but we still have the original as HERE on the stack. That
0035DAr 3                               ; address now has to go where addr2 was before.
0035DAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr-t u-t here here addr1 )
0035DDr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_store            ; ! ( addr-t u-t here )
0035E0r 3               
0035E0r 3                               ; Thus concludes the mucking about with node links. Now we have
0035E0r 3                               ; to create a new header. The CP we access with HERE points to
0035E0r 3                               ; the cell after the new node address, which is where we want
0035E0r 3                               ; to put ( ) for the new string
0035E0r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_here             ; HERE ( addr-t u-t here here2)
0035E3r 3               
0035E3r 3                               ; Reserve two cells (four bytes on the 65c02) for the ( addr u )
0035E3r 3                               ; of the new string
0035E3r 3  A5 00                        lda cp
0035E5r 3  18                           clc
0035E6r 3  69 04                        adc #04
0035E8r 3  85 00                        sta cp
0035EAr 3  90 02                        bcc :+
0035ECr 3  E6 01                        inc cp+1
0035EEr 3               :
0035EEr 3                               ; HERE now points to after the new header. Since we're really
0035EEr 3                               ; going to add something, we can increase the current line
0035EEr 3                               ; number
0035EEr 3  E6 30                        inc ed_cur
0035F0r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
0035F2r 3  E6 31                        inc ed_cur+1
0035F4r 3               :
0035F4r 3                               ; We have the new line sitting in ( cib ciblen ) and need to
0035F4r 3                               ; a) move it somewhere safe and b) get ready for the next
0035F4r 3                               ; line. We arrive here with ( addr-t u-t here here2 ), where here2
0035F4r 3                               ; is where the new string needs to be. The MOVE command we're
0035F4r 3                               ; going to use has the format ( addr1 addr2 u )
0035F4r 3               
0035F4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_here     ; HERE ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 )
0035F7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup      ; DUP ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 here3 )
0035FAr 3               
0035FAr 3  CA                           dex
0035FBr 3  CA                           dex             ; ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 here3 ? )
0035FCr 3  A5 08                        lda cib
0035FEr 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
003600r 3  A5 09                        lda cib+1
003602r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x         ; ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 here3 cib )
003604r 3               
003604r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap     ; SWAP ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 cib here3 )
003607r 3               
003607r 3  CA                           dex
003608r 3  CA                           dex             ; ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 cib here3 ? )
003609r 3  A5 0A                        lda ciblen
00360Br 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
00360Dr 3  A5 0B                        lda ciblen+1
00360Fr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x         ; ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 cib here3 ciblen )
003611r 3               
003611r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_move     ; ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 )
003614r 3               
003614r 3                               ; We need to adjust CP be the length of the string
003614r 3  18                           clc
003615r 3  A5 00                        lda cp
003617r 3  65 0A                        adc ciblen
003619r 3  85 00                        sta cp
00361Br 3  90 06                        bcc :+
00361Dr 3  A5 01                        lda cp+1
00361Fr 3  65 0B                        adc ciblen+1
003621r 3  85 01                        sta cp+1
003623r 3               :
003623r 3                               ; The string is now moved safely out of the input buffer to the
003623r 3                               ; main memory at ( here3 ciblin ). Now we have to fix that
003623r 3                               ; fact in the header. We start with the address.
003623r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over             ; OVER ( addr-t u-t here here2 here3 here2 )
003626r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_store            ; ! ( addr-t u-t here here2 )
003629r 3               
003629r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+
00362Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+ ( addr-t u-t here here2+2 )
00362Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-t u-t here here2+2 here2+2 )
003632r 3               
003632r 3  A5 0A                        lda ciblen
003634r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
003636r 3  A5 0B                        lda ciblen+1
003638r 3  95 03                        sta 3,x                 ; ( addr-t u-t here ciblen here2+2 )
00363Ar 3               
00363Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_store            ; ! ( addr-t u-t here )
00363Dr 3               
00363Dr 3                               ; Add a line feed for visuals
00363Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003640r 3               
003640r 3                               ; Remeber that original HERE we've been dragging along all the
003640r 3                               ; time? Now we find out why. We return to the loop to pick up
003640r 3                               ; the next input
003640r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _next_string_loop
003643r 3               
003643r 3               ; -------------------------
003643r 3               _cmd_d:
003643r 3                       ; d -- Delete one or more lines. This might have to be coded as
003643r 3                       ; a subroutine because other commands such as 'c' might be easier to
003643r 3                       ; implement that way. Note that a lot of this code is very similar to
003643r 3                       ; the loop for 'p'. We arrive here with ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 )
003643r 3  FA                           plx
003644r 3               
003644r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _have_text
003647r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _no_line_zero
00364Ar 3               
00364Ar 3                               ; At least the first line is valid. Most common case is one
00364Ar 3                               ; line, so we check to see if we even have a second parameter.
00364Ar 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00364Cr 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00364Er 3  D0 08                        bne :+
003650r 3               
003650r 3                               ; The second parameter is a zero, so delete one line
003650r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over             ; ( addr-t u-t para1 0 para1 )
003653r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _cmd_d_common       ; ( addr-t u-t para1 0 )
003656r 3  80 33                        bra _cmd_d_done
003658r 3               :
003658r 3                               ; We have been given a range. Make sure that the second
003658r 3                               ; parameter is legal. We arrive here with ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 )
003658r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _is_valid_line      ; result is in C flag
00365Br 3  B0 03                        bcs _cmd_d_loop
00365Dr 3               
00365Dr 3                               ; para2 is not valid. Complain and abort
00365Dr 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _error_2drop
003660r 3               
003660r 3               _cmd_d_loop:
003660r 3                               ; Seems to be a legal range. Walk through and delete If para1
003660r 3                               ; is larger than para2, we're done. Note that Unix ed throws an
003660r 3                               ; error if we start out that way, we might do that in future as
003660r 3                               ; well. This is not the same code as for 'p', because we have
003660r 3                               ; to delete from the back
003660r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_two_dup          ; 2DUP ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 para1 para2 )
003663r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_greater_than     ; > ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 f )
003666r 3               
003666r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
003668r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00366Ar 3  D0 0D                        bne _cmd_d_done_with_flag
00366Cr 3               
00366Cr 3                               ; Para2 is still larger or the same size as para1, so we
00366Cr 3                               ; continue
00366Cr 3  E8                           inx
00366Dr 3  E8                           inx                     ; Get rid of the flag from >
00366Er 3               
00366Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 para2 )
003671r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _cmd_d_common       ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 )
003674r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_minus        ; 1- ( addr-t u-t para1 para2-1 )
003677r 3               
003677r 3  80 E7                        bra _cmd_d_loop
003679r 3               
003679r 3               _cmd_d_done_with_flag:
003679r 3  E8                           inx                     ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 )
00367Ar 3  E8                           inx
00367Br 3               
00367Br 3                               ; The current line is set to the first line minus
00367Br 3                               ; one. Since we don't accept '0d', this at least
00367Br 3                               ; hast to be one
00367Br 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
00367Dr 3  D0 02                        bne :+
00367Fr 3  D6 03                        dec 3,x
003681r 3               :
003681r 3  D6 02                        dec 2,x
003683r 3               
003683r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
003685r 3  85 30                        sta ed_cur
003687r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
003689r 3  85 31                        sta ed_cur+1            ; drop through to _cmd_d_done
00368Br 3               
00368Br 3               _cmd_d_done:
00368Br 3                               ; Text has changed, set flag
00368Br 3  A9 40                        lda #%01000000
00368Dr 3  04 32                        tsb ed_flags
00368Fr 3               
00368Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003692r 3               
003692r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _next_command
003695r 3               
003695r 3               _cmd_d_common:
003695r 3                       ; Internal subroutine to delete a single line when given the line
003695r 3                       ; number TOS. Consumes TOS. What we do is take the link to the next
003695r 3                       ; node and put it in the previous node. The caller is responsible
003695r 3                       ; for setting ed_changed. We arrive here with ( u )
003695r 3               
003695r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-t u-t u u )
003698r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _num_to_addr        ; ( addr-t u-t u addr )
00369Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; @ ( addr-t u-t u addr1 )
00369Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr-t u-t addr1 u )
0036A1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_minus        ; 1- ( addr-t u-t addr1 u-1 )
0036A4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _num_to_addr        ; ( addr-t u-t addr1 addr-1 )
0036A7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_store            ; ! ( addr-t u-t )
0036AAr 3               
0036AAr 3  60                           rts
0036ABr 3               
0036ABr 3               ; -------------------------
0036ABr 3               _cmd_equ:
0036ABr 3                       ; = --- Print the given line number or the current line number if no
0036ABr 3                       ; value is given. This is useful if you want to know what the number of
0036ABr 3                       ; the last line is ("$=")
0036ABr 3  FA                           plx
0036ACr 3               
0036ACr 3                               ; If we don't have a text, we follow Unix ed's example and
0036ACr 3                               ; print a zero. It would seem to make more sense to throw an
0036ACr 3                               ; error, but who are we to argue with Unix.
0036ACr 3  A5 2E                        lda ed_head
0036AEr 3  05 2F                        ora ed_head+1
0036B0r 3  D0 08                        bne _cmd_equ_have_text
0036B2r 3               
0036B2r 3                               ; Fake it: load 0 as para2 and then print. The 0 goes in a new
0036B2r 3                               ; line just like with Unix ed
0036B2r 3  CA                           dex
0036B3r 3  CA                           dex
0036B4r 3  74 00                        stz 0,x
0036B6r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x                 ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 0 )
0036B8r 3  80 21                        bra _cmd_equ_done
0036BAr 3               
0036BAr 3               _cmd_equ_have_text:
0036BAr 3                               ; We have taken care of the case where we don't have a text. If
0036BAr 3                               ; we have a line zero, it is explicit, and we don't do that
0036BAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _no_line_zero
0036BDr 3               
0036BDr 3                               ; If we have no parameters, just print the current line number
0036BDr 3  24 32                        bit ed_flags
0036BFr 3  30 0C                        bmi _cmd_equ_have_para
0036C1r 3               
0036C1r 3  CA                           dex
0036C2r 3  CA                           dex                     ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 ? )
0036C3r 3  A5 30                        lda ed_cur
0036C5r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0036C7r 3  A5 31                        lda ed_cur+1
0036C9r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x
0036CBr 3               
0036CBr 3  80 0E                        bra _cmd_equ_done       ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 cur )
0036CDr 3               
0036CDr 3               _cmd_equ_have_para:
0036CDr 3                               ; We have at least one parameter, and we know it is not zero.
0036CDr 3                               ; We follow the behavior of Unix ed here: If there is one
0036CDr 3                               ; parameter, we print its line number. If there are two
0036CDr 3                               ; separated by a comma (etc), we print the second line number
0036CDr 3                               ; of the range
0036CDr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0036CFr 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
0036D1r 3  D0 05                        bne _cmd_equ_two_paras
0036D3r 3               
0036D3r 3                               ; We've got one parameter
0036D3r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over             ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 para1)
0036D6r 3  80 03                        bra _cmd_equ_done
0036D8r 3               
0036D8r 3               _cmd_equ_two_paras:
0036D8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 para2) drop through
0036DBr 3               
0036DBr 3               _cmd_equ_done:
0036DBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr               ; number goes on new line
0036DEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot            ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 )
0036E1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
0036E4r 3               
0036E4r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _next_command
0036E7r 3               
0036E7r 3               
0036E7r 3               ; -------------------------
0036E7r 3               _cmd_f:
0036E7r 3                       ; f -- Print the address that a write command ("w") will go to or set
0036E7r 3                       ; it. If no parameter was passed, we print the address we have on hand,
0036E7r 3                       ; with a parameter, we set that to the new address. We accept a zero,
0036E7r 3                       ; though that would be a weird place to write, but we do need a text
0036E7r 3  FA                           plx
0036E8r 3               
0036E8r 3  24 32                        bit ed_flags
0036EAr 3  30 17                        bmi _cmd_f_have_para
0036ECr 3               
0036ECr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
0036EFr 3               
0036EFr 3                               ; No parameters, just a naked "f", so print the address buried
0036EFr 3                               ; at the fourth position of the stack: We arrive here with
0036EFr 3                               ; ( addr-t u-t 0 0 )
0036EFr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_to_r             ; >R   ( addr-t u-t 0 ) ( R: 0 )
0036F2r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_rot              ; ROT  ( u-t 0 addr-t ) ( R: 0 )
0036F5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP  ( u-t 0 addr-t addr-t ) ( R: 0 )
0036F8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot            ; U.   ( u-t 0 addr-t ) ( R: 0 )
0036FBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_not_rote         ; -ROT ( addr-t u-t 0 ) ( R: 0 )
0036FEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_r_from           ; R>   ( addr-t u-t 0 0 )
003701r 3               
003701r 3  80 11                        bra _cmd_f_done
003703r 3               
003703r 3               _cmd_f_have_para:
003703r 3                               ; We do no sanity tests at all. This is Forth, if the user
003703r 3                               ; wants to blow up the Zero Page and the Stack, sure, go right
003703r 3                               ; ahead, whatever.
003703r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over
003706r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003709r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot
00370Cr 3               
00370Cr 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
00370Er 3  95 06                        sta 6,x
003710r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
003712r 3  95 07                        sta 7,x                 ; fall through to _cmd_f_done
003714r 3               
003714r 3               _cmd_f_done:
003714r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003717r 3               
003717r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _next_command
00371Ar 3               
00371Ar 3               
00371Ar 3               ; -------------------------
00371Ar 3               _cmd_i:
00371Ar 3                       ; i --- Add text before current line. We allow '0i' and 'i' just like
00371Ar 3                       ; the Unix ed. Note that this routine just prepares the line numbers so
00371Ar 3                       ; we can reuse most of the code from a.
00371Ar 3  FA                           plx
00371Br 3               
00371Br 3                               ; We don't care about para2, because i just adds stuff before
00371Br 3                               ; the line we were given.
00371Br 3  E8                           inx
00371Cr 3  E8                           inx                     ;  DROP ( addr-t u-t para1 )
00371Dr 3               
00371Dr 3                               ; If we weren't given a parameter, make the current line the
00371Dr 3                               ; parameter
00371Dr 3  24 32                        bit ed_flags
00371Fr 3  30 08                        bmi _cmd_i_have_para
003721r 3               
003721r 3                               ; No parameter, take current line
003721r 3  A5 30                        lda ed_cur
003723r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
003725r 3  A5 31                        lda ed_cur+1
003727r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x                 ;  ( addr-t u-t cur ) drop through
003729r 3               
003729r 3               _cmd_i_have_para:
003729r 3                               ; If the parameter is zero, we skip the next part and behave
003729r 3                               ; completely like the "a" command
003729r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00372Br 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00372Dr 3  F0 09                        beq _cmd_i_done
00372Fr 3               
00372Fr 3                               ; We have some other line number, so we start one above it
00372Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_minus        ; 1-  ( addr-t u-t para1-1 )
003732r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_zero             ; 0   ( addr-t u-t para1-1 0 )
003735r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_max              ; MAX ( addr-t u-t para1-1 | 0 )
003738r 3               _cmd_i_done:
003738r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _entry_cmd_i
00373Br 3               
00373Br 3               
00373Br 3               ; -------------------------
00373Br 3               _cmd_n:
00373Br 3                       ; n -- Print lines with a line number. We just set a flag here and
00373Br 3                       ; let p do all the heavy work.
00373Br 3               
00373Br 3  FA                           plx
00373Cr 3               
00373Cr 3  A9 01                        lda #%00000001
00373Er 3  04 32                        tsb ed_flags
003740r 3               
003740r 3  80 05                        bra _cmd_p_entry_for_cmd_n
003742r 3               
003742r 3               
003742r 3               ; -------------------------
003742r 3               _cmd_p:
003742r 3                       ; p -- Print lines without line numbers. This routine is also used
003742r 3                       ; by n, the difference is in a flag. Note that this routine is
003742r 3                       ; able to handle line numbers greater than 255 even though it's
003742r 3                       ; hard to believe somebody would actually use this editor for anything
003742r 3                       ; that long. I'm really sure Leo Tolstoy would not have created "War
003742r 3                       ; and Peace" on a 65c02.
003742r 3               
003742r 3  FA                           plx
003743r 3               
003743r 3               _cmd_p_from_external:
003743r 3                               ; This is coming from p, the variant without line numbers. We
003743r 3                               ; set the ed_flags' bit 0 to zero to mark this
003743r 3  A9 01                        lda #%00000001
003745r 3  14 32                        trb ed_flags
003747r 3               
003747r 3               _cmd_p_entry_for_cmd_n:
003747r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _have_text
00374Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _no_line_zero
00374Dr 3               
00374Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003750r 3               
003750r 3                               ; We now know that there is some number in para1. The most
003750r 3                               ; common case is that para2 is zero and we're being asked to
003750r 3                               ; print a single line
003750r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
003752r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
003754r 3  D0 08                        bne _cmd_p_loop
003756r 3               
003756r 3                               ; Print a single line and be done with it. We could use
003756r 3                               ; DROP here and leave immediately but we want this routine
003756r 3                               ; to have a single exit at the bottom.
003756r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over             ; OVER ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 para1 )
003759r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _cmd_p_common       ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 )
00375Cr 3               
00375Cr 3  80 1E                        bra _cmd_p_all_done
00375Er 3               
00375Er 3               _cmd_p_loop:
00375Er 3                               ; We are being asked to print more than one line, which
00375Er 3                               ; is a bit trickier. If para1 is larger than para2, we're
00375Er 3                               ; done. Note that Unix ed throws an error if we start out
00375Er 3                               ; that way, we might do that in future as well
00375Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_two_dup          ; 2DUP ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 para1 para2 )
003761r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_greater_than     ; > ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 f )
003764r 3               
003764r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
003766r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
003768r 3  D0 10                        bne _cmd_p_done
00376Ar 3               
00376Ar 3                               ; Para2 is still larger or the same size as para1, so we
00376Ar 3                               ; continue
00376Ar 3  E8                           inx
00376Br 3  E8                           inx                     ; Get rid of the flag from >
00376Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over             ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 para1 )
00376Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _cmd_p_common       ; ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 )
003772r 3               
003772r 3  F6 02                        inc 2,x
003774r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
003776r 3  F6 03                        inc 3,x
003778r 3               :
003778r 3  80 E4                        bra _cmd_p_loop
00377Ar 3               
00377Ar 3               _cmd_p_done:
00377Ar 3                               ; We arrive here with ( addr-t u-t para1 para2 f )
00377Ar 3  E8                           inx
00377Br 3  E8                           inx                     ; fall through to _cmp_p_all_done
00377Cr 3               _cmd_p_all_done:
00377Cr 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _next_command
00377Fr 3               
00377Fr 3               
00377Fr 3               _cmd_p_common:
00377Fr 3                       ; Internal subroutine to print a single line when given the line number
00377Fr 3                       ; TOS. Consumes TOS. Used by both n and p. We arrive here with
00377Fr 3                       ; ( addr-t u-t para1 ) as the line number
00377Fr 3               
00377Fr 3                               ; See if we're coming from p (no line numbers, ed_flag is zero)
00377Fr 3                               ; or from n (line numbers and a TAB, ed_flag is $FF)
00377Fr 3  A5 32                        lda ed_flags
003781r 3  4A                           lsr                     ; bit 0 now in carry
003782r 3  90 0B                        bcc _cmd_p_common_no_num
003784r 3               
003784r 3                               ; Bit 0 is set, this is coming from n. Print the line number
003784r 3                               ; followed by a tab
003784r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-t u-t para1 para1 )
003787r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot            ; U. ( addr-t u-t para1 )
00378Ar 3               
00378Ar 3  A9 09                        lda #$09                 ; ASCII for Tab
00378Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr emit_a
00378Fr 3               
00378Fr 3               _cmd_p_common_no_num:
00378Fr 3                               ; One way or the other, print the the node's string
00378Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _num_to_addr        ; ( addr-t u-t addr )
003792r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _print_addr
003795r 3               
003795r 3  60                           rts
003796r 3               
003796r 3               
003796r 3               ; -------------------------
003796r 3               _cmd_q:
003796r 3                       ; q -- Quit if all work as been saved, complain otherwise
003796r 3               
003796r 3  FA                           plx
003797r 3               
003797r 3  24 32                        bit ed_flags            ; bit 6 is change flag
003799r 3  50 03                        bvc :+
00379Br 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _error_2drop
00379Er 3               :
00379Er 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _all_done            ; can't fall thru because of PLX
0037A1r 3               
0037A1r 3               
0037A1r 3               ; -------------------------
0037A1r 3               _cmd_qq:
0037A1r 3                       ; Q -- Quit unconditionally, dumping any work that is unsaved
0037A1r 3                       ; without any warning. We can't just jump to all done because
0037A1r 3                       ; of the PLX
0037A1r 3  FA                           plx
0037A2r 3               
0037A2r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _all_done
0037A5r 3               
0037A5r 3               
0037A5r 3               ; -------------------------
0037A5r 3               _cmd_w:
0037A5r 3                       ; w --- Write text to system memory. In contrast to the Unix ed word,
0037A5r 3                       ; we provide the address before the command, such as "8000w". If no
0037A5r 3                       ; address is given -- just 'w' -- we write to whatever was fixed with
0037A5r 3                       ; 'f'. To prevent a common, but potentially common error, we do not
0037A5r 3                       ; allow writing to the first page ($0000 to $00FF) unless the address
0037A5r 3                       ; was specificially passed as a parameter. Currently, we can only enter
0037A5r 3                       ; the address in decimal.
0037A5r 3  FA                           plx
0037A6r 3               
0037A6r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _have_text
0037A9r 3               
0037A9r 3  24 32                        bit ed_flags
0037ABr 3  30 13                        bmi _cmd_w_have_para
0037ADr 3               
0037ADr 3                               ; If we don't have a parameter, we check what is stored on the
0037ADr 3                               ; stack and use that address -- UNLESS IT IS 0000, which is
0037ADr 3                               ; what it would be if the user wasn't thinking and just pressed
0037ADr 3                               ; 'w' at the beginning. We arrive here with ( addr-t u-t 0 0 )
0037ADr 3  B5 06                        lda 6,x
0037AFr 3  15 07                        ora 7,x
0037B1r 3  D0 03                        bne :+
0037B3r 3               
0037B3r 3                               ; It's a zero, generate an error to protect the users from
0037B3r 3                               ; themselves
0037B3r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _error_2drop
0037B6r 3               :
0037B6r 3                               ; Not a zero, we assume user knows what they are doing. Get the
0037B6r 3                               ; address.
0037B6r 3  B5 06                        lda 6,x
0037B8r 3  95 02                        sta 2,x
0037BAr 3  B5 07                        lda 7,x
0037BCr 3  95 03                        sta 3,x                 ; ( addr-t u-t addr-t ? )
0037BEr 3               
0037BEr 3  80 08                        bra _cmd_w_para_ready
0037C0r 3               
0037C0r 3               _cmd_w_have_para:
0037C0r 3                               ; We were given a parameter, which we now make the new
0037C0r 3                               ; default parameter. This is different from Unix w, where
0037C0r 3                               ; the filename set by f is not changed by w
0037C0r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
0037C2r 3  95 06                        sta 6,x
0037C4r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
0037C6r 3  95 07                        sta 7,x                 ; drop through to _cmd_w_para_ready
0037C8r 3               
0037C8r 3               _cmd_w_para_ready:
0037C8r 3                               ; We don't care about the second parameter, the first one must
0037C8r 3                               ; be an address. There is actually no way to test if this is an
0037C8r 3                               ; address because any number could be a 16-bit address. Anyway,
0037C8r 3                               ; we overwrite para2 with the address where the pointer to the
0037C8r 3                               ; first entry in the list is kept.
0037C8r 3  A9 2E                        lda #<ed_head
0037CAr 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0037CCr 3  A9 00                        lda #>ed_head
0037CEr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x                 ; ( addr-t u-t addr-t addr-h )
0037D0r 3               
0037D0r 3                               ; We need to keep a copy of the original target address to
0037D0r 3                               ; calculate how many chars (including carriage returns) we
0037D0r 3                               ; saved at the end of this routine
0037D0r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_over             ; OVER ( addr-t u-t addr-t addr-h addr-t )
0037D3r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_to_r             ; >R ( addr-t u-t addr-t addr-h ) ( R: addr-t )
0037D6r 3               
0037D6r 3               _cmd_w_loop:
0037D6r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; @ ( addr-t u-t addr-t addr1 ) ( R: addr-t )
0037D9r 3               
0037D9r 3                               ; If we're at the end of the list, quit. For the next block of
0037D9r 3                               ; text, we ignore the ( addr-t u-t ) at the beginning
0037D9r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0037DBr 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
0037DDr 3  F0 55                        beq _cmd_w_eol
0037DFr 3               
0037DFr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_two_dup          ; 2DUP ( addr-t addr-1 addr-t addr-1 ) ( R: addr-t addr-1 addr-t )
0037E2r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_two_to_r         ; 2>R  ( addr-t addr-1 ) (R: ... )
0037E5r 3               
0037E5r 3                               ; Get the address and length of the string from the header
0037E5r 3                               ; of this node
0037E5r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+ ( addr-t addr1+1 ) (R: ... )
0037E8r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+ ( addr-t addr1+2 ) (R: ... )
0037EBr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-t addr1+2 addr1+2 ) ( R: ... )
0037EEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; @ ( addr-t addr1+2 addr-s ) ( R: ... )
0037F1r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr-t addr-s addr1+2 ) ( R: ... )
0037F4r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+ ( addr-t addr-s addr1+1 ) (R: ... )
0037F7r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+ ( addr-t addr-s addr1+2 ) (R: ... )
0037FAr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; @ ( addr-t addr-s u-s ) ( R: ... )
0037FDr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_not_rote         ; -ROT ( u-s addr-t addr-s ) ( R: ... )
003800r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( u-s addr-s addr-t ) ( R: ... )
003803r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_rot              ; ROT (addr-s addr-t u-s ) ( R: ... )
003806r 3               
003806r 3                               ; We need a copy of the string length u-s to adjust the pointer
003806r 3                               ; to the store area later
003806r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP (addr-s addr-t u-s u-s ) ( R: ... )
003809r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_to_r             ; >R (addr-s addr-t u-s ) ( R: ... u-s )
00380Cr 3               
00380Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_move             ; MOVE ( )( R: addr-t addr-1 addr-t )
00380Fr 3               
00380Fr 3                               ; Calculate the position of the next string in the save area.
00380Fr 3                               ; What we don't do is remember the length of the individual
00380Fr 3                               ; strings; instead at the end we will subtract addresses to
00380Fr 3                               ; get the length of the string
00380Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_r_from           ; R> ( u-s )  ( R: addr-t addr-h addr-t )
003812r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_two_r_from       ; 2R> ( u-s addr-t addr-h ) ( R: addr-t )
003815r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_not_rote         ; -ROT ( addr-h u-s addr-t ) ( R: addr-t )
003818r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_plus             ; + ( addr-h addr-t1 ) ( R: addr-t )
00381Br 3               
00381Br 3                               ; But wait, our strings are terminated by Line Feeds in
00381Br 3                               ; memory, so we need to add one
00381Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-h addr-t1 addr-t1 ) ( R: addr-t )
00381Er 3               
00381Er 3  CA                           dex
00381Fr 3  CA                           dex                     ; ( addr-h addr-t1 addr-t1 ? ) ( R: addr-t )
003820r 3  A9 0A                        lda #AscLF              ; ASCII for LF
003822r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
003824r 3  74 01                        stz 1,x                 ; ( addr-h addr-t1 addr-t1 c ) ( R: addr-t )
003826r 3               
003826r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr-h addr-t1 c addr-t1 ) ( R: addr-t )
003829r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_store            ; ! ( addr-h addr-t1 ) ( R: addr-t )
00382Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; 1+ ( addr-h addr-t1+1 ) ( R: addr-t )
00382Fr 3               
00382Fr 3                               ; Now we can handle the next line
00382Fr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr-t1+1 addr-h ) ( R: addr-t )
003832r 3               
003832r 3  80 A2                        bra _cmd_w_loop
003834r 3               
003834r 3               _cmd_w_eol:
003834r 3                               ; We're at the end of the text buffer and arrive here with
003834r 3                               ; ( addr-tn addr-n ) ( R: addr-t ) What we do now is calculate
003834r 3                               ; the number of characters saved and put that value in the 3OS
003834r 3                               ; position
003834r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr-t u-t addr-n addr-tn ) ( R: addr-t )
003837r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_r_from           ; R> ( addr-t u-t addr-n addr-tn addr-t )
00383Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_minus            ; - ( addr-t u-t addr-n u )
00383Dr 3               
00383Dr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00383Fr 3  95 04                        sta 4,x
003841r 3  B5 01                        lda 1,x
003843r 3  95 05                        sta 5,x                 ; ( addr-t u addr-n u )
003845r 3               
003845r 3                               ; Unix ed puts the number of characters on a new line, so we
003845r 3                               ; do as well
003845r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003848r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; DUP ( addr-t u addr-n u u )
00384Br 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_u_dot            ; U. ( addr-t u addr-n u )
00384Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003851r 3               
003851r 3                               ; Reset the changed flag
003851r 3  A9 40                        lda #%01000000
003853r 3  14 32                        trb ed_flags
003855r 3               
003855r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _next_command
003858r 3               
003858r 3               
003858r 3               ; === ERROR HANDLING ===
003858r 3               
003858r 3               _error_2drop:
003858r 3                               ; Lots of times we'll have para1 and para2 on the stack when an
003858r 3                               ; error occurs, so we drop stuff here
003858r 3  E8                           inx
003859r 3  E8                           inx                     ; drop through to _error_1drop
00385Ar 3               _error_1drop:
00385Ar 3  E8                           inx
00385Br 3  E8                           inx                     ; drop through to _error
00385Cr 3               _error:
00385Cr 3                               ; Error handling with ed is really primitive: We print a question
00385Cr 3                               ; mark and go back to the loop. Any code calling this routine must
00385Cr 3                               ; clean up the stack itself: We expect it to be empty. Note that
00385Cr 3                               ; ed currently does not support reporting the type of error on
00385Cr 3                               ; demand like Unix ed does
00385Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
00385Fr 3               
00385Fr 3  A9 3F                        lda #'?'
003861r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr emit_a
003864r 3               
003864r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
003867r 3               
003867r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _input_loop
00386Ar 3               
00386Ar 3               
00386Ar 3               ; === HELPER FUNCTIONS ===
00386Ar 3               
00386Ar 3               _get_input:
00386Ar 3                       ; Use REFILL to get input from the user, which is left in
00386Ar 3                       ; ( cib ciblen ) as usual.
00386Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_refill           ;  ( addr-t u-t f )
00386Dr 3               
00386Dr 3                               ; If something went wrong while getting the user input, print
00386Dr 3                               ; a question mark and try again. No fancy error messages
00386Dr 3                               ; for ed!
00386Dr 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00386Fr 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
003871r 3  D0 05                        bne :+
003873r 3               
003873r 3                               ; Whatever went wrong, we can't handle it here anyway. We
003873r 3                               ; clear the return stack, dump the error flag and call
003873r 3                               ; a normal error
003873r 3  7A                           ply
003874r 3  7A                           ply
003875r 3               
003875r 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _error_1drop
003878r 3               :
003878r 3                               ; Drop the flag
003878r 3  E8                           inx
003879r 3  E8                           inx
00387Ar 3               
00387Ar 3  60                           rts
00387Br 3               
00387Br 3               ; -----------------------------
00387Br 3               _have_text:
00387Br 3                       ; See if we have any lines at all. If not, abort with an error. We
00387Br 3                       ; could in theory set a flag every time we add a text, but this is
00387Br 3                       ; more robust, if somewhat longer
00387Br 3  A5 2E                        lda ed_head
00387Dr 3  05 2F                        ora ed_head+1
00387Fr 3  D0 04                        bne :+
003881r 3               
003881r 3                               ; We don't have any lines. Clean up the return stack and throw
003881r 3                               ; an error
003881r 3  7A                           ply
003882r 3  7A                           ply
003883r 3  80 D7                        bra _error
003885r 3               :
003885r 3  60                           rts
003886r 3               
003886r 3               ; -----------------------------
003886r 3               _is_valid_line:
003886r 3                       ; See if the line number in TOS is valid. If yes, returns the carry
003886r 3                       ; flag set ("true"), otherwise cleared ("false"). Does not change
003886r 3                       ; the value of TOS. Line numbers must be 0 < number <= last_line.
003886r 3                       ; This routine calls _last_line.
003886r 3  38                           sec                             ; default is legal line number
003887r 3               
003887r 3                               ; First see if we have a zero
003887r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
003889r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00388Br 3  F0 19                        beq _is_valid_line_nope_zero    ; ( n )
00388Dr 3               
00388Dr 3                               ; Not a zero. Now see if we're beyond the last line
00388Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup                      ; DUP ( n n )
003890r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr _last_line                  ; ( n n last )
003893r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap                     ; SWAP ( n last n )
003896r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_less_than                ; < ( n f )
003899r 3               
003899r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x                         ; 0 flag is good
00389Br 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00389Dr 3  D0 05                        bne _is_valid_line_too_small
00389Fr 3               
00389Fr 3                               ; We're good, clean up and leave
00389Fr 3  E8                           inx
0038A0r 3  E8                           inx                     ; DROP flag ( n )
0038A1r 3               
0038A1r 3  38                           sec                     ; Who knows what's happened to C by now
0038A2r 3  80 03                        bra _is_valid_line_done ; only one exit from this routine
0038A4r 3               
0038A4r 3               _is_valid_line_too_small:
0038A4r 3  E8                           inx
0038A5r 3  E8                           inx                     ; drop through to _is_valid_line_zero
0038A6r 3               
0038A6r 3               _is_valid_line_nope_zero:
0038A6r 3  18                           clc                     ; drop through to _is_valid_line_done
0038A7r 3               
0038A7r 3               _is_valid_line_done:
0038A7r 3  60                           rts
0038A8r 3               
0038A8r 3               
0038A8r 3               ; -----------------------------
0038A8r 3               _last_line:
0038A8r 3                       ; Calculate the number of the last line (not its address) and return
0038A8r 3                       ; it TOS. Note this shares code with _num_to_addr. Assumes that user
0038A8r 3                       ; has made sure there are any lines at all
0038A8r 3               
0038A8r 3                               ; Set counter to zero
0038A8r 3  64 24                        stz tmp1
0038AAr 3  64 25                        stz tmp1+1
0038ACr 3               
0038ACr 3  CA                           dex
0038ADr 3  CA                           dex                     ; ( ? )
0038AEr 3  A9 2E                        lda #<ed_head
0038B0r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0038B2r 3  A9 00                        lda #>ed_head
0038B4r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x                 ; ( addr )
0038B6r 3               
0038B6r 3               _last_line_loop:
0038B6r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; ( addr | 0 )
0038B9r 3               
0038B9r 3                               ; If that's over, we're at the end of the list and we're done
0038B9r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0038BBr 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
0038BDr 3  F0 08                        beq _last_line_done
0038BFr 3               
0038BFr 3                               ; Not done. Increase counter and continue
0038BFr 3  E6 24                        inc tmp1
0038C1r 3  D0 02                        bne :+
0038C3r 3  E6 25                        inc tmp1+1
0038C5r 3               :
0038C5r 3  80 EF                        bra _last_line_loop
0038C7r 3               
0038C7r 3               _last_line_done:
0038C7r 3  A5 24                        lda tmp1
0038C9r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0038CBr 3  A5 25                        lda tmp1+1
0038CDr 3  95 01                        sta 1,x                 ; ( u )
0038CFr 3               
0038CFr 3  60                           rts
0038D0r 3               
0038D0r 3               
0038D0r 3               ; -----------------------------
0038D0r 3               _no_line_zero:
0038D0r 3                       ; Make sure we weren't given an explicit zero as the line number with
0038D0r 3                       ; commands that don't accept it (that is, pretty much everybody except
0038D0r 3                       ; a). If para1 is a zero and we have parameters (bit 7 of ed_flag set),
0038D0r 3                       ; throw an error
0038D0r 3               
0038D0r 3                               ; See if para1 is zero
0038D0r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
0038D2r 3  15 03                        ora 3,x
0038D4r 3  D0 07                        bne _no_line_zero_done
0038D6r 3               
0038D6r 3                               ; It's zero. If bit 7 of ed_flag is set, this is an explicit
0038D6r 3                               ; parameter
0038D6r 3  24 32                        bit ed_flags
0038D8r 3  10 03                        bpl _no_line_zero_done
0038DAr 3               
0038DAr 3  4C rr rr                     jmp _error_2drop
0038DDr 3               
0038DDr 3               _no_line_zero_done:
0038DDr 3                               ; All is well, we can continue
0038DDr 3  60                           rts
0038DEr 3               
0038DEr 3               ; -----------------------------
0038DEr 3               _num_to_addr:
0038DEr 3                       ; Given a line number as TOS, replace it by the address of the node.
0038DEr 3                       ; If the line number is zero, we return the address of the header
0038DEr 3                       ; node. If the line number is beyond the last line, we return a
0038DEr 3                       ; zero, though we're assuming the user will check for a legal
0038DEr 3                       ; line number before calling this routine. Assumes we have checked that
0038DEr 3                       ; we have any text at all.
0038DEr 3               
0038DEr 3                               ; One way or another we're going to start with the
0038DEr 3                               ; address of the pointer to the head of the list
0038DEr 3  CA                           dex
0038DFr 3  CA                           dex                     ; ( u ? )
0038E0r 3  A9 2E                        lda #<ed_head
0038E2r 3  95 00                        sta 0,x
0038E4r 3  A9 00                        lda #>ed_head
0038E6r 3  95 01                        sta 1,x                 ; ( u addr-h )
0038E8r 3               
0038E8r 3                               ; Handle the case where the line number is zero
0038E8r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
0038EAr 3  15 03                        ora 3,x
0038ECr 3  D0 05                        bne _num_to_addr_loop
0038EEr 3               
0038EEr 3                               ; It's zero, so we're already done
0038EEr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_nip              ; ( addr-h )
0038F1r 3  80 21                        bra _num_to_addr_done
0038F3r 3               
0038F3r 3               _num_to_addr_loop:
0038F3r 3                               ; Get the first line
0038F3r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; @ ( u addr1 )
0038F6r 3               
0038F6r 3                               ; If that's zero, we're at the end of the list and it's over
0038F6r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
0038F8r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
0038FAr 3  D0 05                        bne :+
0038FCr 3               
0038FCr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_nip              ; NIP ( addr1 )
0038FFr 3  80 13                        bra _num_to_addr_done
003901r 3               :
003901r 3                               ; It's not zero. See if this is the nth element we're looking
003901r 3                               ; for
003901r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( addr1 u )
003904r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_minus        ; 1- ( addr1 u-1 )
003907r 3               
003907r 3  B5 00                        lda 0,x
003909r 3  15 01                        ora 1,x
00390Br 3  F0 05                        beq _num_to_addr_finished
00390Dr 3               
00390Dr 3                               ; Not zero yet, try again
00390Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; SWAP ( u-1 addr1 )
003910r 3               
003910r 3  80 E1                        bra _num_to_addr_loop
003912r 3               
003912r 3               _num_to_addr_finished:
003912r 3                               ; We arrive here with ( addr u )
003912r 3  E8                           inx
003913r 3  E8                           inx                     ; ( addr )
003914r 3               
003914r 3               _num_to_addr_done:
003914r 3  60                           rts
003915r 3               
003915r 3               
003915r 3               ; -----------------------------
003915r 3               _para1_to_cur:
003915r 3                       ; Switch the current line number to whatever the first parameter
003915r 3                       ; is. We do this a lot so this routine saves a few bytes
003915r 3  B5 02                        lda 2,x
003917r 3  85 30                        sta ed_cur
003919r 3  B5 03                        lda 3,x
00391Br 3  85 31                        sta ed_cur+1
00391Dr 3               
00391Dr 3  60                           rts
00391Er 3               
00391Er 3               
00391Er 3               ; -----------------------------
00391Er 3               _print_addr:
00391Er 3                       ; Given the address of a node TOS, print the string it comes with.
00391Er 3                       ; Assumes we have made sure that this address exists. It would be
00391Er 3                       ; nice to put the CR at the beginning, but that doesn't work with
00391Er 3                       ; the n commands, so at the end it goes. Consumes TOS.
00391Er 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus
003921r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; ( addr+2 )
003924r 3               
003924r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_dup              ; ( addr+2 addr+2 )
003927r 3               
003927r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus
00392Ar 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_one_plus         ; ( addr+2 addr+4 )
00392Dr 3               
00392Dr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; ( addr+2 u-s )
003930r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; ( u-s addr+2 )
003933r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_fetch            ; ( u-s addr-s )
003936r 3               
003936r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap             ; ( addr-s u-s )
003939r 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_type
00393Cr 3  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
00393Fr 3               
00393Fr 3  60                           rts
003940r 3               
003940r 3               
003940r 3               ; === COMMAND TABLES ===
003940r 3               
003940r 3               ; The commands are all one character and kept in a 0-terminated string that is
003940r 3               ; walked by a loop. Their index corresponds to the index of their routine's
003940r 3               ; address in the jump table. To create a new command, add it's letter at the
003940r 3               ; correct position in the command list and the routine's address in the command
003940r 3               ; jump table. Oh, and write the routine as well. Capital letters such as 'Q' are
003940r 3               ; coded in their routine's address as double letters ('_cmd_qq').
003940r 3               
003940r 3  61 66 69 64  ed_cmd_list:    .byte "afidpn=wqQ", 0
003944r 3  70 6E 3D 77  
003948r 3  71 51 00     
00394Br 3               
00394Br 3               ed_cmd_table:
00394Br 3  rr rr rr rr                  .word _cmd_a, _cmd_f, _cmd_i, _cmd_d, _cmd_p, _cmd_n
00394Fr 3  rr rr rr rr  
003953r 3  rr rr rr rr  
003957r 3  rr rr rr rr                  .word _cmd_equ, _cmd_w, _cmd_q, _cmd_qq
00395Br 3  rr rr rr rr  
00395Fr 3               
00395Fr 3               .endscope
00395Fr 3               ed6502_end:     ; Used to calculate size of editor code
00395Fr 3               
00395Fr 2               
00395Fr 2               ; High-level Forth words, see forth_code/README.md
00395Fr 2               forth_words_start:
00395Fr 2  20 63 72 20  .incbin "forth_words.asc"
003963r 2  2E 28 20 54  
003967r 2  61 6C 69 20  
003A23r 2               forth_words_end:
003A23r 2               
003A23r 2               ; User-defined Forth words, see forth_code/README.md
003A23r 2               user_words_start:
003A23r 2  20 20        .incbin "user_words.asc"
003A25r 2               user_words_end:
003A25r 2               
003A25r 2               .include "headers.asm"          ; Headers of native words
003A25r 3               ; Dictionary Headers for Tali Forth 2
003A25r 3               ; Scot W. Stevenson <scot.stevenson@gmail.com>
003A25r 3               ; First version: 05. Dec 2016 (Liara Forth)
003A25r 3               ; This version: 29. Dec 2018
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ; Dictionary headers are kept separately from the code, which allows various
003A25r 3               ; tricks in the code. We roughly follow the Gforth terminology: The Execution
003A25r 3               ; Token (xt) is the address of the first byte of a word's code that can be, uh,
003A25r 3               ; executed; the Name Token (nt) is a pointer to the beginning of the word's
003A25r 3               ; header in the Dictionary. There the link to the next word in the Dictionary
003A25r 3               ; is always one cell down from the current word's own nt. In the code itself,
003A25r 3               ; we use "nt_<WORD>" for the nt and "xt_<WORD>" for the xt.
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ; This gives us the following header structure:
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ;              8 bit     8 bit
003A25r 3               ;               LSB       MSB
003A25r 3               ; nt_word ->  +--------+--------+
003A25r 3               ;          +0 | Length | Status |
003A25r 3               ;             +--------+--------+
003A25r 3               ;          +2 | Next Header     | -> nt_next_word
003A25r 3               ;             +-----------------+
003A25r 3               ;          +4 | Start of Code   | -> xt_word
003A25r 3               ;             +-----------------+
003A25r 3               ;          +6 | End of Code     | -> z_word
003A25r 3               ;             +--------+--------+
003A25r 3               ;          +8 | Name   |        |
003A25r 3               ;             +--------+--------+
003A25r 3               ;             |        |        |
003A25r 3               ;             +--------+--------+
003A25r 3               ;             |        |  ...   | (name string does not end with a zero)
003A25r 3               ;          +n +--------+--------+
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ; The Status Byte is created by adding the flags defined in definitions.asm,
003A25r 3               ; which are:
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ;       CO - Compile Only
003A25r 3               ;       IM - Immediate Word
003A25r 3               ;       NN - Never Native Compile (must always be called by JSR)
003A25r 3               ;       AN - Always Native Compile (may not be called by JSR)
003A25r 3               ;       UF - Contains underflow check
003A25r 3               ;       HC - Has CFA (words created by CREATE and DOES> only)
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ; Note there are currently two bits unused.
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ; By default, all existing words can be natively compiled (compiled inline) or
003A25r 3               ; as a subroutine jump target; the system decides which variant to use based on
003A25r 3               ; a threshold the user can set. By default, all user-created words are flagged
003A25r 3               ; never-native. The user can override this by using the always-native word
003A25r 3               ; just after defining their new word.  The NN flag forbids native compiling,
003A25r 3               ; the AN flag forces it.
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ; The last word (top word in code) is always BYE. It is marked as the last word
003A25r 3               ; by its value of 0000 in its Next Header field. The words are sorted with the
003A25r 3               ; more common ones first (further down in code) so they are found earlier.
003A25r 3               ; Anything to do with output comes later (further up) because things will
003A25r 3               ; always be slow if there is a human involved.
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               ; The initial skeleton of this list was automatically generated by a script
003A25r 3               ; in the tools folder and then sorted by hand.
003A25r 3               
003A25r 3               nt_bye:
003A25r 3  03                   .byte 3         ; length of word strings
003A26r 3  00                   .byte 0         ; status byte
003A27r 3  00 00                .word 0000      ; next word in Dictionary, 0000 signals end
003A29r 3  rr rr                .word xt_bye    ; start of code block (xt of this word)
003A2Br 3  rr rr                .word z_bye     ; end of code (RTS)
003A2Dr 3  62 79 65             .byte "bye"     ; word name, always lower case, not zero-terminated

0048BDr 3               
0048BDr 3               ; ASSEMBLER-WORDLIST
0048BDr 3               
0048BDr 3               ; Labels for the opcodes have the format "nt_asm_<OPC>" where a futher
0048BDr 3               ; underscore replaces any dot present in the SAN mnemonic. The hash sign for
0048BDr 3               ; immediate addressing is replaced by an "h" (for example, the label code for
0048BDr 3               ; "lda.#" is "xt_adm_lda_h"). All opcodes are immediate.
0048BDr 3               assembler_dictionary_start:
0048BDr 3               nt_asm_adc_h:
0048BDr 3  05 04        		.byte 5, IM
0048BFr 3  rr rr                        .word nt_asm_adc_x
0048C1r 3  rr rr rr rr  		.word xt_asm_adc_h, z_asm_adc_h
0048C5r 3  61 64 63 2E  		.byte "adc.#"
0048C9r 3  23           
0048CAr 3

0051A7r 2               .include "strings.asm"          ; Strings, including error messages
0051A7r 3               ; List of Strings for Tali Forth 2
0051A7r 3               ; Scot W. Stevenson <scot.stevenson@gmail.com>
0051A7r 3               ; First version: 01. Apr 2016 (for Liara Forth)
0051A7r 3               ; This version: 28. Dec 2018
0051A7r 3               
0051A7r 3               ; This file is included by taliforth.asm
0051A7r 3               
0051A7r 3               ; ## GENERAL STRINGS
0051A7r 3               
0051A7r 3               ; All general strings must be zero-terminated, names start with "s_",
0051A7r 3               ; aliases with "str_"
0051A7r 3               
0051A7r 3               str_ok             =  0
0051A7r 3               str_compile        =  1
0051A7r 3               str_redefined      =  2
0051A7r 3               str_wid_forth      =  3
0051A7r 3               str_abc_lower      =  4
0051A7r 3               str_abc_upper      =  5
0051A7r 3               str_wid_editor     =  6
0051A7r 3               str_wid_assembler  =  7
0051A7r 3               str_wid_root       =  8
0051A7r 3               str_see_flags      =  9
0051A7r 3               str_see_nt         = 10
0051A7r 3               str_see_xt         = 11
0051A7r 3               str_see_size       = 12
0051A7r 3               
0051A7r 3               ; Since we can't fit a 16-bit address in a register, we use indexes as offsets
0051A7r 3               ; to tables as error and string numbers.
0051A7r 3               string_table:

005279r 3               ; ## ERROR STRINGS
005279r 3               
005279r 3               ; All error strings must be zero-terminated, all names start with "es_",
005279r 3               ; aliases with "err_". If the string texts are changed, the test suite must be
005279r 3               ; as well
005279r 3

005279r 3               err_compileonly  = 2
