00532Br 3  66 61 69 6C  
005333r 3  51 55 49 54  es_refill:      .byte "QUIT could not get input (REFILL returned -1)", 0
005337r 3  20 63 6F 75  
00533Br 3  6C 64 20 6E  
005361r 3  41 6C 72 65  es_state:       .byte "Already in compile mode", 0
005365r 3  61 64 79 20  
005369r 3  69 6E 20 63  
005379r 3  55 6E 64 65  es_syntax:      .byte "Undefined word", 0
00537Dr 3  66 69 6E 65  
005381r 3  64 20 77 6F  
005388r 3  53 74 61 63  es_underflow:   .byte "Stack underflow", 0
00538Cr 3  6B 20 75 6E  
005390r 3  64 65 72 66  
005398r 3  4D 61 78 20  es_negallot:    .byte "Max memory freed with ALLOT", 0
00539Cr 3  6D 65 6D 6F  
0053A0r 3  72 79 20 66  
0053B4r 3  4E 6F 20 77  es_wordlist:    .byte "No wordlists available", 0
0053B8r 3  6F 72 64 6C  
0053BCr 3  69 73 74 73  
0053CBr 3  50 6C 65 61  es_blockwords:  .byte "Please assign vectors BLOCK-READ-VECTOR and BLOCK-WRITE-VECTOR",0
0053CFr 3  73 65 20 61  
0053D3r 3  73 73 69 67  
00540Ar 3               
00540Ar 3               ; ## ENVIRONMENT STRINGS
00540Ar 3               
00540Ar 3               ; These are used by the ENVIRONMENT? word and stored in the old string format:
00540Ar 3               ; Length byte first, then the string itself that is not rpt. not
00540Ar 3               ; zero-terminated. Note these are uppercase by ANS defintion. All start with
00540Ar 3               ; "envs_".
00540Ar 3               
00540Ar 3               ; These return a single-cell number
00540Ar 3  0F 2F 43 4F  envs_cs:        .byte 15, "/COUNTED-STRING"
00540Er 3  55 4E 54 45  
005412r 3  44 2D 53 54  
00541Ar 3  05 2F 48 4F  envs_hold:      .byte 5, "/HOLD"
00541Er 3  4C 44        
005420r 3  04 2F 50 41  envs_pad:       .byte 4, "/PAD"
005424r 3  44           
005425r 3  11 41 44 44  envs_aub:       .byte 17, "ADDRESS-UNIT-BITS"
005429r 3  52 45 53 53  
00542Dr 3  2D 55 4E 49  
005437r 3  07 46 4C 4F  envs_floored:   .byte 7, "FLOORED"
00543Br 3  4F 52 45 44  
00543Fr 3  08 4D 41 58  envs_max_char:  .byte 8, "MAX-CHAR"
005443r 3  2D 43 48 41  
005447r 3  52           
005448r 3  05 4D 41 58  envs_max_n:     .byte 5, "MAX-N"
00544Cr 3  2D 4E        
00544Er 3  05 4D 41 58  envs_max_u:     .byte 5, "MAX-U"
005452r 3  2D 55        
005454r 3  12 52 45 54  envs_rsc:       .byte 18, "RETURN-STACK-CELLS"
005458r 3  55 52 4E 2D  
00545Cr 3  53 54 41 43  
005467r 3  0B 53 54 41  envs_sc:        .byte 11, "STACK-CELLS"
00546Br 3  43 4B 2D 43  
00546Fr 3  45 4C 4C 53  
005473r 3  09 57 4F 52  envs_wl:        .byte 9, "WORDLISTS"
005477r 3  44 4C 49 53  
00547Br 3  54 53        
00547Dr 3               
00547Dr 3               ; These return a double-cell number
00547Dr 3  05 4D 41 58  envs_max_d:     .byte 5, "MAX-D"
005481r 3  2D 44        
005483r 3  06 4D 41 58  envs_max_ud:    .byte 6, "MAX-UD"
005487r 3  2D 55 44     
00548Ar 3               
00548Ar 3               ; END
00548Ar 3               
00548Ar 2               
00548Ar 2               
00548Ar 2               ; =====================================================================
00548Ar 2               ; COMPILE WORDS, JUMPS and SUBROUTINE JUMPS INTO CODE
00548Ar 2               
00548Ar 2               ; These three routines compile instructions such as "jsr xt_words" into a word
00548Ar 2               ; at compile time so they are available at run time. Words that use this
00548Ar 2               ; routine may not be natively compiled. We use "cmpl" as not to confuse these
00548Ar 2               ; routines with the COMPILE, word. Always call this with a subroutine jump.
00548Ar 2               ; This means combining JSR/RTS to JMP in those cases is not going to work. To
00548Ar 2               ; use, load the LSB of the address in A and the MSB in Y. You can remember
00548Ar 2               ; which comes first by thinking of the song "Young Americans" ("YA") by David
00548Ar 2               ; Bowie.
00548Ar 2               
00548Ar 2               ;               ldy #>addr      ; MSB   ; "Young"
00548Ar 2               ;               lda #<addr      ; LSB   ; "Americans"
00548Ar 2               ;               jsr cmpl_subroutine
00548Ar 2               
00548Ar 2               ; Also, we keep a routine here to compile a single byte passed through A.
00548Ar 2               
00548Ar 2               cmpl_subroutine:
00548Ar 2                               ; This is the entry point to compile JSR <ADDR>
00548Ar 2  48                           pha             ; save LSB of address
00548Br 2  A9 20                        lda #$20        ; load opcode for JSR
00548Dr 2  80 03                        bra cmpl_common
00548Fr 2               cmpl_jump:
00548Fr 2                               ; This is the entry point to compile JMP <ADDR>
00548Fr 2  48                           pha             ; save LSB of address
005490r 2  A9 4C                        lda #$4c        ; load opcode for JMP, fall thru to cmpl_common
005492r 2               cmpl_common:
005492r 2                               ; At this point, A contains the opcode to be compiled,
005492r 2                               ; the LSB of the address is on the 65c02 stack, and the MSB of
005492r 2                               ; the address is in Y
005492r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_a      ; compile opcode
005495r 2  68                           pla             ; retrieve address LSB; fall thru to cmpl_word
005496r 2               cmpl_word:
005496r 2                               ; This is the entry point to compile a word (little-endian)
005496r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_a      ; compile LSB of address
005499r 2  98                           tya             ; fall thru for MSB
00549Ar 2               cmpl_a:
00549Ar 2               .scope
00549Ar 2                               ; This is the entry point to compile a single byte which
00549Ar 2                               ; is passed in A. The built-in assembler assumes that this
00549Ar 2                               ; routine does not modify Y.
00549Ar 2  92 00                        sta (cp)
00549Cr 2  E6 00                        inc cp
00549Er 2  D0 02                        bne _done
0054A0r 2  E6 01                        inc cp+1
0054A2r 2               _done:
0054A2r 2  60                           rts
0054A3r 2               .endscope
0054A3r 2               
0054A3r 2               
0054A3r 2               ; =====================================================================
0054A3r 2               ; CODE FIELD ROUTINES
0054A3r 2               
0054A3r 2               doconst:
0054A3r 2                       ; """Execute a CONSTANT: Push the data in the first two bytes of
0054A3r 2                       ; the Data Field onto the Data Stack
0054A3r 2                       ; """
0054A3r 2  CA                           dex             ; make room for constant
0054A4r 2  CA                           dex
0054A5r 2               
0054A5r 2                               ; The value we need is stored in the two bytes after the
0054A5r 2                               ; JSR return address, which in turn is what is on top of
0054A5r 2                               ; the Return Stack
0054A5r 2  68                           pla             ; LSB of return address
0054A6r 2  85 24                        sta tmp1
0054A8r 2  68                           pla             ; MSB of return address
0054A9r 2  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
0054ABr 2               
0054ABr 2                               ; Start LDY with 1 instead of 0 because of how JSR stores
0054ABr 2                               ; the return address on the 65c02
0054ABr 2  A0 01                        ldy #1
0054ADr 2  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y
0054AFr 2  95 00                        sta 0,x
0054B1r 2  C8                           iny
0054B2r 2  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y
0054B4r 2  95 01                        sta 1,x
0054B6r 2               
0054B6r 2                               ; This takes us back to the original caller, not the
0054B6r 2                               ; DOCONST caller
0054B6r 2  60                           rts
0054B7r 2               
0054B7r 2               
0054B7r 2               dodefer:
0054B7r 2                       ; """Execute a DEFER statement at runtime: Execute the address we
0054B7r 2                       ; find after the caller in the Data Field
0054B7r 2                       ; """
0054B7r 2                               ; The xt we need is stored in the two bytes after the JSR
0054B7r 2                               ; return address, which is what is on top of the Return
0054B7r 2                               ; Stack. So all we have to do is replace our return jump
0054B7r 2                               ; with what we find there
0054B7r 2  68                           pla             ; LSB
0054B8r 2  85 24                        sta tmp1
0054BAr 2  68                           pla             ; MSB
0054BBr 2  85 25                        sta tmp1+1
0054BDr 2               
0054BDr 2  A0 01                        ldy #1
0054BFr 2  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y
0054C1r 2  85 26                        sta tmp2
0054C3r 2  C8                           iny
0054C4r 2  B1 24                        lda (tmp1),y
0054C6r 2  85 27                        sta tmp2+1
0054C8r 2               
0054C8r 2  6C 26 00                     jmp (tmp2)      ; This is actually a jump to the new target
0054CBr 2               
0054CBr 2               defer_error:
0054CBr 2                               ; """Error routine for undefined DEFER: Complain and abort"""
0054CBr 2  A9 03                        lda #err_defer
0054CDr 2  4C rr rr                     jmp error
0054D0r 2               
0054D0r 2               dodoes:
0054D0r 2                       ; """Execute the runtime portion of DOES>. See DOES> and
0054D0r 2                       ; docs/create-does.txt for details and
0054D0r 2                       ; http://www.bradrodriguez.com/papers/moving3.htm
0054D0r 2                       ; """
0054D0r 2                               ; Assumes the address of the CFA of the original defining word
0054D0r 2                               ; (say, CONSTANT) is on the top of the Return Stack. Save it
0054D0r 2                               ; for a later jump, adding one byte because of the way the
0054D0r 2                               ; 6502 works
0054D0r 2  7A                           ply             ; LSB
0054D1r 2  68                           pla             ; MSB
0054D2r 2  C8                           iny
0054D3r 2  D0 01                        bne :+
0054D5r 2  1A                           inc
0054D6r 2               :
0054D6r 2  84 26                        sty tmp2
0054D8r 2  85 27                        sta tmp2+1
0054DAr 2               
0054DAr 2                               ; Next on the Return Stack should be the address of the PFA of
0054DAr 2                               ; the calling defined word (say, the name of whatever constant we
0054DAr 2                               ; just defined). Move this to the Data Stack, again adding one.
0054DAr 2  CA                           dex
0054DBr 2  CA                           dex
0054DCr 2               
0054DCr 2  7A                           ply
0054DDr 2  68                           pla
0054DEr 2  C8                           iny
0054DFr 2  D0 01                        bne :+
0054E1r 2  1A                           inc
0054E2r 2               :
0054E2r 2  94 00                        sty 0,x         ; LSB
0054E4r 2  95 01                        sta 1,x         ; MSB
0054E6r 2               
0054E6r 2                               ; This leaves the return address from the original main routine
0054E6r 2                               ; on top of the Return Stack. We leave that untouched and jump
0054E6r 2                               ; to the special code of the defining word. It's RTS instruction
0054E6r 2                               ; will take us back to the main routine
0054E6r 2  6C 26 00                     jmp (tmp2)
0054E9r 2               
0054E9r 2               
0054E9r 2               dovar:
0054E9r 2                       ; """Execute a variable: Push the address of the first bytes of
0054E9r 2                       ; the Data Field onto the stack. This is called with JSR so we
0054E9r 2                       ; can pick up the address of the calling variable off the 65c02's
0054E9r 2                       ; stack. The final RTS takes us to the original caller of the
0054E9r 2                       ; routine that itself called DOVAR. This is the default
0054E9r 2                       ; routine installed with CREATE.
0054E9r 2                       ; """
0054E9r 2                               ; Pull the return address off the machine's stack, adding
0054E9r 2                               ; one because of the way the 65c02 handles subroutines
0054E9r 2  7A                           ply             ; LSB
0054EAr 2  68                           pla             ; MSB
0054EBr 2  C8                           iny
0054ECr 2  D0 01                        bne :+
0054EEr 2  1A                           inc
0054EFr 2               :
0054EFr 2  CA                           dex
0054F0r 2  CA                           dex
0054F1r 2               
0054F1r 2  95 01                        sta 1,x
0054F3r 2  98                           tya
0054F4r 2  95 00                        sta 0,x
0054F6r 2               
0054F6r 2  60                           rts
0054F7r 2               
0054F7r 2               ; =====================================================================
0054F7r 2               ; LOW LEVEL HELPER FUNCTIONS
0054F7r 2               
0054F7r 2               byte_to_ascii:
0054F7r 2                       ; """Convert byte in A to two ASCII hex digits and EMIT them"""
0054F7r 2               .scope
0054F7r 2  48                           pha
0054F8r 2  4A                           lsr             ; convert high nibble first
0054F9r 2  4A                           lsr
0054FAr 2  4A                           lsr
0054FBr 2  4A                           lsr
0054FCr 2  20 rr rr                     jsr _nibble_to_ascii
0054FFr 2  68                           pla
005500r 2               
005500r 2                               ; fall through to _nibble_to_ascii
005500r 2               
005500r 2               _nibble_to_ascii:
005500r 2                       ; """Private helper function for byte_to_ascii: Print lower nibble
005500r 2                       ; of A and and EMIT it. This does the actual work.
005500r 2                       ; """
005500r 2  29 0F                        and #$0F
005502r 2  09 30                        ora #'0'
005504r 2  C9 3A                        cmp #$3A        ; '9+1
005506r 2  90 02                        bcc :+
005508r 2  69 06                        adc #$06
00550Ar 2               
00550Ar 2  4C rr rr     :               jmp emit_a
00550Dr 2               
00550Dr 2  60                           rts
00550Er 2               .endscope
00550Er 2               
00550Er 2               compare_16bit:
00550Er 2                       ; """Compare TOS/NOS and return results in form of the 65c02 flags
00550Er 2                       ; Adapted from Leventhal "6502 Assembly Language Subroutines", see
00550Er 2                       ; also http://www.6502.org/tutorials/compare_beyond.html
00550Er 2                       ; For signed numbers, Z signals equality and N which number is larger:
00550Er 2                       ;       if TOS = NOS: Z=1 and N=0
00550Er 2                       ;       if TOS > NOS: Z=0 and N=0
00550Er 2                       ;       if TOS < NOS: Z=0 and N=1
00550Er 2                       ; For unsigned numbers, Z signals equality and C which number is larger:
00550Er 2                       ;       if TOS = NOS: Z=1 and N=0
00550Er 2                       ;       if TOS > NOS: Z=0 and C=1
00550Er 2                       ;       if TOS < NOS: Z=0 and C=0
00550Er 2                       ; Compared to the book routine, WORD1 (MINUED) is TOS
00550Er 2                       ;                               WORD2 (SUBTRAHEND) is NOS
00550Er 2                       ; """
00550Er 2               .scope
00550Er 2                               ; Compare LSB first to set the carry flag
00550Er 2  B5 00                        lda 0,x                 ; LSB of TOS
005510r 2  D5 02                        cmp 2,x                 ; LSB of NOS
005512r 2  F0 0A                        beq _equal
005514r 2               
005514r 2                               ; LSBs are not equal, compare MSB
005514r 2  B5 01                        lda 1,x                 ; MSB of TOS
005516r 2  F5 03                        sbc 3,x                 ; MSB of NOS
005518r 2  09 01                        ora #1                  ; Make zero flag 0 because not equal
00551Ar 2  70 08                        bvs _overflow
00551Cr 2  80 08                        bra _not_equal
00551Er 2               _equal:
00551Er 2                               ; Low bytes are equal, so we compare high bytes
00551Er 2  B5 01                        lda 1,x                 ; MSB of TOS
005520r 2  F5 03                        sbc 3,x                 ; MSB of NOS
005522r 2  50 04                        bvc _done
005524r 2               _overflow:
005524r 2                               ; Handle overflow because we use signed numbers
005524r 2  49 80                        eor #$80                ; complement negative flag
005526r 2               _not_equal:
005526r 2  09 01                        ora #1                  ; if overflow, we can't be eqal
005528r 2               _done:
005528r 2  60                           rts
005529r 2               .endscope
005529r 2               
005529r 2               current_to_dp:
005529r 2                       ; """Look up the current (compilation) dictionary pointer
005529r 2                       ; in the wordlist set and put it into the dp zero-page
005529r 2                       ; variable. Uses A and Y.
005529r 2                       ; """
005529r 2                               ; Determine which wordlist is current
005529r 2  A0 04                        ldy #current_offset
00552Br 2  B1 1E                        lda (up),y      ; current is a byte variable
00552Dr 2  0A                           asl             ; turn it into an offset (in cells)
00552Er 2               
00552Er 2                               ; Get the dictionary pointer for that wordlist.
00552Er 2  18                           clc
00552Fr 2  69 06                        adc #wordlists_offset   ; add offset to wordlists base.
005531r 2  A8                           tay
005532r 2  B1 1E                        lda (up),y              ; get the dp for that wordlist.
005534r 2  85 02                        sta dp
005536r 2  C8                           iny
005537r 2  B1 1E                        lda (up),y
005539r 2  85 03                        sta dp+1
00553Br 2               
00553Br 2  60                           rts
00553Cr 2               
00553Cr 2               
00553Cr 2               dp_to_current:
00553Cr 2                       ; """Look up which wordlist is current and update its pointer
00553Cr 2                       ; with the value in dp. Uses A and Y.
00553Cr 2                       ; """
00553Cr 2                               ; Determine which wordlist is current
00553Cr 2  A0 04                        ldy #current_offset
00553Er 2  B1 1E                        lda (up),y      ; current is a byte variable
005540r 2  0A                           asl             ; turn it into an offset (in cells)
005541r 2               
005541r 2                               ; Get the dictionary pointer for that wordlist.
005541r 2  18                           clc
005542r 2  69 06                        adc #wordlists_offset   ; add offset to wordlists base.
005544r 2  A8                           tay
005545r 2  A5 02                        lda dp
005547r 2  91 1E                        sta (up),y              ; get the dp for that wordlist.
005549r 2  C8                           iny
00554Ar 2  A5 03                        lda dp+1
00554Cr 2  91 1E                        sta (up),y
00554Er 2               
00554Er 2  60                           rts
00554Fr 2               
00554Fr 2               interpret:
00554Fr 2               .scope
00554Fr 2                       ; """Core routine for the interpreter called by EVALUATE and QUIT.
00554Fr 2                       ; Process one line only. Assumes that the address of name is in
00554Fr 2                       ; cib and the length of the whole input line string is in ciblen
00554Fr 2                       ; """
00554Fr 2                               ; Normally we would use PARSE here with the SPACE character as
00554Fr 2                               ; a parameter (PARSE replaces WORD in modern Forths). However,
00554Fr 2                               ; Gforth's PARSE-NAME makes more sense as it uses spaces as
00554Fr 2                               ; delimiters per default and skips any leading spaces, which
00554Fr 2                               ; PARSE doesn't
00554Fr 2               _loop:
00554Fr 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_parse_name       ; ( "string" -- addr u )
005552r 2               
005552r 2                               ; If PARSE-NAME returns 0 (empty line), no characters were left
005552r 2                               ; in the line and we need to go get a new line
005552r 2  B5 00                        lda 0,x
005554r 2  15 01                        ora 1,x
005556r 2  F0 70                        beq _line_done
005558r 2               
005558r 2                               ; Go to FIND-NAME to see if this is a word we know. We have to
005558r 2                               ; make a copy of the address in case it isn't a word we know and
005558r 2                               ; we have to go see if it is a number
005558r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_two_dup          ; ( addr u -- addr u addr u )
00555Br 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_find_name        ; ( addr u addr u -- addr u nt|0 )
00555Er 2               
00555Er 2                               ; A zero signals that we didn't find a word in the Dictionary
00555Er 2  B5 00                        lda 0,x
005560r 2  15 01                        ora 1,x
005562r 2  D0 28                        bne _got_name_token
005564r 2               
005564r 2                               ; We didn't get any nt we know of, so let's see if this is
005564r 2                               ; a number.
005564r 2  E8                           inx                     ; ( addr u 0 -- addr u )
005565r 2  E8                           inx
005566r 2               
005566r 2                               ; If the number conversion doesn't work, NUMBER will do the
005566r 2                               ; complaining for us
005566r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_number           ; ( addr u -- u|d )
005569r 2               
005569r 2                               ; Otherweise, if we're interpreting, we're done
005569r 2  A5 16                        lda state
00556Br 2  F0 E2                        beq _loop
00556Dr 2               
00556Dr 2                               ; We're compiling, so there is a bit more work.  Check
00556Dr 2                               ; status bit 5 to see if it's a single or double-cell
00556Dr 2                               ; number.
00556Dr 2  A9 20                        lda #$20
00556Fr 2  24 20                        bit status
005571r 2  F0 0D                        beq _single_number
005573r 2               
005573r 2                               ; It's a double cell number.  If we swap the
005573r 2                               ; upper and lower half, we can use the literal_runtime twice
005573r 2                               ; to compile it into the dictionary.
005573r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_swap
005576r 2  A0 rr                        ldy #>literal_runtime
005578r 2  A9 rr                        lda #<literal_runtime
00557Ar 2  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_subroutine
00557Dr 2               
00557Dr 2                               ; compile our number
00557Dr 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_comma
005580r 2               
005580r 2                               ; Fall into _single_number to process the other half.
005580r 2               _single_number:
005580r 2  A0 rr                        ldy #>literal_runtime
005582r 2  A9 rr                        lda #<literal_runtime
005584r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr cmpl_subroutine
005587r 2               
005587r 2                               ; compile our number
005587r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_comma
00558Ar 2               
00558Ar 2                               ; That was so much fun, let's do it again!
00558Ar 2  80 C3                        bra _loop
00558Cr 2               
00558Cr 2               _got_name_token:
00558Cr 2                               ; We have a known word's nt TOS. We're going to need its xt
00558Cr 2                               ; though, which is four bytes father down.
00558Cr 2               
00558Cr 2                               ; We arrive here with ( addr u nt ), so we NIP twice
00558Cr 2  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00558Er 2  95 04                        sta 4,x
005590r 2  B5 01                        lda 1,x
005592r 2  95 05                        sta 5,x
005594r 2               
005594r 2  E8                           inx
005595r 2  E8                           inx
005596r 2  E8                           inx
005597r 2  E8                           inx                     ; ( nt )
005598r 2               
005598r 2                               ; Save a version of nt for error handling and compilation stuff
005598r 2  B5 00                        lda 0,x
00559Ar 2  85 22                        sta tmpbranch
00559Cr 2  B5 01                        lda 1,x
00559Er 2  85 23                        sta tmpbranch+1
0055A0r 2               
0055A0r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_name_to_int      ; ( nt - xt )
0055A3r 2               
0055A3r 2                               ; See if we are in interpret or compile mode, 0 is interpret
0055A3r 2  A5 16                        lda state
0055A5r 2  D0 13                        bne _compile
0055A7r 2               
0055A7r 2                               ; We are interpreting, so EXECUTE the xt that is TOS. First,
0055A7r 2                               ; though, see if this isn't a compile-only word, which would be
0055A7r 2                               ; illegal. The status byte is the second one of the header.
0055A7r 2  A0 01                        ldy #1
0055A9r 2  B1 22                        lda (tmpbranch),y
0055ABr 2  29 01                        and #CO                 ; mask everything but Compile Only bit
0055ADr 2  F0 05                        beq _interpret
0055AFr 2               
0055AFr 2  A9 02                        lda #err_compileonly
0055B1r 2  4C rr rr                     jmp error
0055B4r 2               
0055B4r 2               _interpret:
0055B4r 2                               ; We JSR to EXECUTE instead of calling the xt directly because
0055B4r 2                               ; the RTS of the word we're executing will bring us back here,
0055B4r 2                               ; skipping EXECUTE completely during RTS. If we were to execute
0055B4r 2                               ; xt directly, we have to fool around with the Return Stack
0055B4r 2                               ; instead, which is actually slightly slower
0055B4r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_execute
0055B7r 2               
0055B7r 2                               ; That's quite enough for this word, let's get the next one
0055B7r 2  4C rr rr                     jmp _loop
0055BAr 2               
0055BAr 2               _compile:
0055BAr 2                               ; We're compiling! However, we need to see if this is an
0055BAr 2                               ; IMMEDIATE word, which would mean we execute it right now even
0055BAr 2                               ; during compilation mode. Fortunately, we saved the nt so life
0055BAr 2                               ; is easier. The flags are in the second byte of the header
0055BAr 2  A0 01                        ldy #1
0055BCr 2  B1 22                        lda (tmpbranch),y
0055BEr 2  29 04                        and #IM                 ; Mask all but IM bit
0055C0r 2  D0 F2                        bne _interpret          ; IMMEDIATE word, execute right now
0055C2r 2               
0055C2r 2                               ; Compile the xt into the Dictionary with COMPILE,
0055C2r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_compile_comma
0055C5r 2  4C rr rr                     jmp _loop
0055C8r 2               
0055C8r 2               _line_done:
0055C8r 2                               ; drop stuff from PARSE_NAME
0055C8r 2  E8                           inx
0055C9r 2  E8                           inx
0055CAr 2  E8                           inx
0055CBr 2  E8                           inx
0055CCr 2               
0055CCr 2  60                           rts
0055CDr 2               .endscope
0055CDr 2               
0055CDr 2               
0055CDr 2               is_printable:
0055CDr 2               .scope
0055CDr 2                       ; """Given a character in A, check if it is a printable ASCII
0055CDr 2                       ; character in the range from $20 to $7E inclusive. Returns the
0055CDr 2                       ; result in the Carry Flag: 0 (clear) is not printable, 1 (set)
0055CDr 2                       ; is printable. Keeps A. See
0055CDr 2                       ; http://www.obelisk.me.uk/6502/algorithms.html for a
0055CDr 2                       ; discussion of various ways to do this
0055CDr 2  C9 20                        cmp #AscSP              ; $20
0055CFr 2  90 08                        bcc _done
0055D1r 2  C9 7F                        cmp #'~' + 1             ; $7E
0055D3r 2  B0 03                        bcs _failed
0055D5r 2               
0055D5r 2  38                           sec
0055D6r 2  80 01                        bra _done
0055D8r 2               _failed:
0055D8r 2  18                           clc
0055D9r 2               _done:
0055D9r 2  60                           rts
0055DAr 2               .endscope
0055DAr 2               
0055DAr 2               
0055DAr 2               is_whitespace:
0055DAr 2               .scope
0055DAr 2                       ; """Given a character in A, check if it is a whitespace
0055DAr 2                       ; character, that is, an ASCII value from 0 to 32 (where
0055DAr 2                       ; 32 is SPACE). Returns the result in the Carry Flag:
0055DAr 2                       ; 0 (clear) is no, it isn't whitespace, while 1 (set) means
0055DAr 2                       ; that it is whitespace. See PARSE and PARSE-NAME for
0055DAr 2                       ; a discussion of the uses. Does not change A or Y.
0055DAr 2  C9 00                        cmp #00         ; explicit comparison to leave Y untouched
0055DCr 2  90 08                        bcc _done
0055DEr 2               
0055DEr 2  C9 21                        cmp #AscSP+1
0055E0r 2  B0 03                        bcs _failed
0055E2r 2               
0055E2r 2  38                           sec
0055E3r 2  80 01                        bra _done
0055E5r 2               _failed:
0055E5r 2  18                           clc
0055E6r 2               _done:
0055E6r 2  60                           rts
0055E7r 2               .endscope
0055E7r 2               
0055E7r 2               
0055E7r 2               ; Underflow tests. We jump to the label with the number of cells (not: bytes)
0055E7r 2               ; required for the word. This routine flows into the generic error handling
0055E7r 2               ; code
0055E7r 2               underflow_1:
0055E7r 2                       ; """Make sure we have at least one cell on the Data Stack"""
0055E7r 2  E0 77                        cpx #dsp0-1
0055E9r 2  10 10                        bpl underflow_error
0055EBr 2  60                           rts
0055ECr 2               underflow_2:
0055ECr 2                       ; """Make sure we have at least two cells on the Data Stack"""
0055ECr 2  E0 75                        cpx #dsp0-3
0055EEr 2  10 0B                        bpl underflow_error
0055F0r 2  60                           rts
0055F1r 2               underflow_3:
0055F1r 2                       ; """Make sure we have at least three cells on the Data Stack"""
0055F1r 2  E0 73                        cpx #dsp0-5
0055F3r 2  10 06                        bpl underflow_error
0055F5r 2  60                           rts
0055F6r 2               underflow_4:
0055F6r 2                       ; """Make sure we have at least four cells on the Data Stack"""
0055F6r 2  E0 71                        cpx #dsp0-7
0055F8r 2  10 01                        bpl underflow_error
0055FAr 2  60                           rts
0055FBr 2               
0055FBr 2               underflow_error:
0055FBr 2                               ; Entry for COLD/ABORT/QUIT
0055FBr 2  A9 09                        lda #err_underflow      ; fall through to error
0055FDr 2               
0055FDr 2               error:
0055FDr 2                       ; """Given the error number in a, print the associated error string and
0055FDr 2                       ; call abort. Uses tmp3.
0055FDr 2                       ; """
0055FDr 2  0A                           asl
0055FEr 2  A8                           tay
0055FFr 2  B9 rr rr                     lda error_table,y
005602r 2  85 28                        sta tmp3                ; LSB
005604r 2  C8                           iny
005605r 2  B9 rr rr                     lda error_table,y
005608r 2  85 29                        sta tmp3+1              ; MSB
00560Ar 2               
00560Ar 2  20 rr rr                     jsr print_common
00560Dr 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_cr
005610r 2  4C rr rr                     jmp xt_abort            ; no jsr, as we clobber return stack
005613r 2               
005613r 2               
005613r 2               ; =====================================================================
005613r 2               ; PRINTING ROUTINES
005613r 2               
005613r 2               ; We distinguish two types of print calls, both of which take the string number
005613r 2               ; (see strings.asm) in A:
005613r 2               
005613r 2               ;       print_string       - with a line feed
005613r 2               ;       print_string_no_lf - without a line feed
005613r 2               
005613r 2               ; In addition, print_common provides a lower-level alternative for error
005613r 2               ; handling and anything else that provides the address of the
005613r 2               ; zero-terminated string directly in tmp3. All of those routines assume that
005613r 2               ; printing should be more concerned with size than speed, because anything to
005613r 2               ; do with humans reading text is going to be slow.
005613r 2               
005613r 2               print_string_no_lf:
005613r 2                       ; """Given the number of a zero-terminated string in A, print it to the
005613r 2                       ; current output without adding a LF. Uses Y and tmp3 by falling
005613r 2                       ; through to print_common
005613r 2                       ; """
005613r 2                               ; Get the entry from the string table
005613r 2  0A                           asl
005614r 2  A8                           tay
005615r 2  B9 rr rr                     lda string_table,y
005618r 2  85 28                        sta tmp3                ; LSB
00561Ar 2  C8                           iny
00561Br 2  B9 rr rr                     lda string_table,y
00561Er 2  85 29                        sta tmp3+1              ; MSB
005620r 2               
005620r 2                               ; fall through to print_common
005620r 2               print_common:
005620r 2                       ; """Common print routine used by both the print functions and
005620r 2                       ; the error printing routine. Assumes string address is in tmp3. Uses
005620r 2                       ; Y.
005620r 2                       ; """
005620r 2               .scope
005620r 2  A0 00                        ldy #0
005622r 2               _loop:
005622r 2  B1 28                        lda (tmp3),y
005624r 2  F0 06                        beq _done               ; strings are zero-terminated
005626r 2               
005626r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr emit_a              ; allows vectoring via output
005629r 2  C8                           iny
00562Ar 2  80 F6                        bra _loop
00562Cr 2               _done:
00562Cr 2  60                           rts
00562Dr 2               .endscope
00562Dr 2               
00562Dr 2               print_string:
00562Dr 2                       ; """Print a zero-terminated string to the console/screen, adding a LF.
00562Dr 2                       ; We do not check to see if the index is out of range. Uses tmp3.
00562Dr 2                       ; """
00562Dr 2  20 rr rr                     jsr print_string_no_lf
005630r 2  4C rr rr                     jmp xt_cr               ; JSR/RTS because never compiled
005633r 2               
005633r 2               
005633r 2               print_u:
005633r 2                       ; """basic printing routine used by higher-level constructs,
005633r 2                       ; the equivalent of the forth word  0 <# #s #> type  which is
005633r 2                       ; basically u. without the space at the end. used for various
005633r 2                       ; outputs
005633r 2                       ; """
005633r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_zero                     ; 0
005636r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_less_number_sign         ; <#
005639r 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_number_sign_s            ; #S
00563Cr 2  20 rr rr                     jsr xt_number_sign_greater      ; #>
00563Fr 2  4C rr rr                     jmp xt_type                     ; JSR/RTS because never compiled
005642r 2               
005642r 2               ; END
005642r 2               
005642r 1               
005642r 1               ; =====================================================================
005642r 1               ; FINALLY
005642r 1               
005642r 1               ; Of the 32 KiB we use, 24 KiB are reserved for Tali (from $8000 to $DFFF)
005642r 1               ; and the last eight (from $E000 to $FFFF) are left for whatever the user
005642r 1               ; wants to use them for.
005642r 1               
005642r 1               
005642r 1               ; Default kernel file for Tali Forth 2
005642r 1               ; Scot W. Stevenson <scot.stevenson@gmail.com>
005642r 1               ; First version: 19. Jan 2014
005642r 1               ; This version: 18. Feb 2018
005642r 1               ;
005642r 1               ; This section attempts to isolate the hardware-dependent parts of Tali
005642r 1               ; Forth 2 to make it easier for people to port it to their own machines.
005642r 1               ; Ideally, you shouldn't have to touch any other files. There are three
005642r 1               ; routines and one string that must be present for Tali to run:
005642r 1               ;
005642r 1               ;       kernel_init - Initialize the low-level hardware
005642r 1               ;       kernel_getc - Get single character in A from the keyboard (blocks)
005642r 1               ;       kernel_putc - Prints the character in A to the screen
005642r 1               ;       s_kernel_id - The zero-terminated string printed at boot
005642r 1               ;
005642r 1               ; This default version Tali ships with is written for the py65mon machine
005642r 1               ; monitor (see docs/MANUAL.md for details).
005642r 1               
005642r 1               ; The main file of Tali got us to $e000. However, py65mon by default puts
005642r 1               ; the basic I/O routines at the beginning of $f000. We don't want to change
005642r 1               ; that because it would make using it out of the box harder, so we just
005642r 1               ; advance past the virtual hardware addresses.  The py65mon.cfg starts
005642r 1               ; this segment at $f010.
005642r 1               .segment "TALIKERNEL"
000000r 1               
000000r 1               ; All vectors currently end up in the same place - we restart the system
000000r 1               ; hard. If you want to use them on actual hardware, you'll have to redirect
000000r 1               ; them all.
000000r 1               v_nmi:
000000r 1               v_reset:
000000r 1               v_irq:
000000r 1               kernel_init:
000000r 1                       ; """Initialize the hardware. This is called with a JMP and not
000000r 1                       ; a JSR because we don't have anything set up for that yet. With
000000r 1                       ; py65mon, of course, this is really easy. -- At the end, we JMP
000000r 1                       ; back to the label forth to start the Forth system.
000000r 1                       ; """
000000r 1               .scope
000000r 1                               ; Since the default case for Tali is the py65mon emulator, we
000000r 1                               ; have no use for interrupts. If you are going to include
000000r 1                               ; them in your system in any way, you're going to have to
000000r 1                               ; do it from scratch. Sorry.
000000r 1  78                           sei             ; Disable interrupts
000001r 1               
000001r 1                               ; We've successfully set everything up, so print the kernel
000001r 1                               ; string
000001r 1  A2 00                        ldx #0
000003r 1  BD rr rr     :               lda s_kernel_id,x
000006r 1  F0 06                        beq _done
000008r 1  20 rr rr                     jsr kernel_putc
00000Br 1  E8                           inx
00000Cr 1  80 F5                        bra :-
00000Er 1               _done:
00000Er 1  4C rr rr                     jmp forth
000011r 1               .endscope
000011r 1               
000011r 1               kernel_getc:
000011r 1                       ; """Get a single character from the keyboard. By default, py65mon
000011r 1                       ; is set to $f004, which we just keep. Note that py65mon's getc routine
000011r 1                       ; is non-blocking, so it will return '00' even if no key has been
000011r 1                       ; pressed. We turn this into a blocking version by waiting for a
000011r 1                       ; non-zero character.
000011r 1                       ; """
000011r 1               .scope
000011r 1               _loop:
000011r 1  AD 04 F0                     lda $f004
000014r 1  F0 FB                        beq _loop
000016r 1  60                           rts
000017r 1               .endscope
000017r 1               
000017r 1               
000017r 1               kernel_putc:
000017r 1                       ; """Print a single character to the console. By default, py65mon
000017r 1                       ; is set to $f001, which we just keep.
000017r 1                       ; """
000017r 1  8D 01 F0                     sta $f001
00001Ar 1  60                           rts
00001Br 1               
00001Br 1               platform_bye:
00001Br 1  00               brk
00001Cr 1               
00001Cr 1               ; Leave the following string as the last entry in the kernel routine so it
00001Cr 1               ; is easier to see where the kernel ends in hex dumps. This string is
00001Cr 1               ; displayed after a successful boot
00001Cr 1               s_kernel_id:
00001Cr 1  54 61 6C 69          .byte "Tali Forth 2 default kernel for py65mon (18. Feb 2018)", AscLF, 0
000020r 1  20 46 6F 72  
000024r 1  74 68 20 32  
000054r 1               
000054r 1               
000054r 1               ; Add the interrupt vectors
000054r 1               .SEGMENT "VECTORS"
000000r 1               
000000r 1  rr rr        .word v_nmi
000002r 1  rr rr        .word v_reset
000004r 1  rr rr        .word v_irq
000006r 1               
000006r 1               ; END
000006r 1               
000006r 1
